currentState:
The scan/audit engine is mature, carefully reasoned, and genuinely well-engineered for a deterministic, offline, never-throw evidence pipeline. Confirmed by reading the actual code:

INGESTION (src/audit-ingest.js, 916 lines): Re-reads provider log shapes (LiteLLM/Helicone/Portkey/OpenRouter chat, OpenAI Responses API, OpenAI Assistants API, Anthropic content-block tool_use) and normalizes each agent action into a canonical AuditEvent, preserving the security dimensions importers throw away (granted tools, invoked tools, egress host, identity, PII/secret content). Real strengths: stable cross-record tool-call dedup via provider call_id (lines 579-582, 741-743) so the same logical call appearing in record N response + record N+1 history is not double-counted; PII/secret scan over tool-call ARGUMENTS not just message text (lines 585-593) closing the primary exfil channel; a reserved KOLM_CAPTURE_SOURCE grade-A path (eventsFromCaptureRow) the routes layer fences (audit-routes.js _normalizeSource/_claimsCaptureSource, lines 134-140) against forged first-party capture claims. ingestForAudit never throws; bad lines become {index,reason} errors.

EVENT SCHEMA (src/audit-event.js): Canonical AuditEvent + a privilege-tier classifier (classifyScopeTier) that ranks the LEADING VERB token, so list_payments/read_emails are correctly tier-1 reads not tier-4 egress, with unknown tokens defaulting to tier-2 (fail-dangerous-direction). Content-derived ids fold a discriminator so parallel calls do not collide into false duplicate findings.

ORCHESTRATION (src/audit-orchestrator.js): Runs 8 analyzers over the SAME events (permission, audit-trail, model-provenance, agent-identity, rag-memory, delegation, egress, memory-integrity) plus a separate red-team/injection battery, each _safeAnalyze never-throw wrapped. The readiness rollup is the standout: explicit non-inflation discipline (untested controls EXCLUDED from the denominator, never silently passed; supplementals only fold in when they surface a hard blocker), a graduated pass=1/attention=0.5/blocking=0 weight, and an A/B/C evidence-tier grade (gateway-capture / hash-verified / asserted) computed where every input is visible. The not_assessed block names ASR-4 and ASR-6 explicitly. This is exactly the 'no theater' posture the product brand demands.

TRAIL ANALYSIS (src/audit-trail-analyzer.js): completeness / tamper-evidence / retention, with two notably careful touches: order-INDEPENDENT hash-chain verification (builds a hash-set and checks each distinct prev-link against it, lines 140-152) to avoid a credibility-destroying critical false positive on newest-first or merged exports; and a deliberately-generous volume-consistency heuristic (GAP-3) that flags the statistical signature of a curated 'quiet week' export and asks for a coverage declaration rather than alleging fraud.

DELTA (src/audit-delta.js): A pure projection over two SIGNED envelopes (reads only signature-covered fields), ASCII-only (so it can be embedded in a signed row), drops raw evidence/detail so a delta never widens PII surface, computes readiness movement + control transitions + findings added/resolved + a regression verdict. Never throws.

EXPORT (src/audit-export.js): SIEM-grade exporters (RFC-4180 CSV, ArcSight CEF, QRadar LEEF, JSONL) with proper per-format escaping, W411 defense-in-depth tenant fencing (all() + explicit per-row tenant_id filter, never findByTenant due to a documented field-key mismatch), row caps, and a 10-row preview cap.

SCORING (src/case-scorer.js, src/comparators.js): These are the EVAL/bench scorer for model artifacts (subset-equal vs deep-equal reconciliation, W345), NOT the security-readiness scorer. They are solid and pure but belong to platform-B eval, not the ASR readiness path.

ROUTES (src/audit-routes.js, 2080 lines): sessions/scan/report surface with a hard revenue gate (tenantHasReportEntitlement), public offline verify with tier-1 (signature) + tier-2 (issuer provenance) checks, rate-limited public verify, framework/OSCAL/badge exports as pure views over the SAME signed envelope.

src/audit.js is a SEPARATE concern: the per-tenant HMAC-chained operational audit LOG (compile/billing/key events), not the agent-scan engine; it is solid (BEGIN IMMEDIATE concurrency fix, canonical JSON, verifyAuditChain).
sota:
Frontier scan (June 2026): The agentic-security-evidence space crystallized around three artifacts the engine should explicitly crosswalk. (1) OWASP Top 10 for Agentic Applications 2026 (genai.owasp.org) - v1.0 due end of 2026, public review opened April 2026; the canonical risk taxonomy buyers will cite. (2) CSA MAESTRO (cloudsecurityalliance.org, Feb 2025) - a 7-layer agentic threat model (Foundation Models, Data Ops, Agent Frameworks, Deployment/Infra, Eval/Observability, Security/Compliance, Agent Ecosystem) with each OWASP-agentic risk mapped to layers. (3) OWASP AIVSS v0.8 (aivss.owasp.org, released March 19 2026 ahead of RSAC) - the big one for this component: it extends CVSS v4.0 with agentic AMPLIFICATION FACTORS (autonomy level, tool-use scope, multi-agent interaction, non-determinism, self-modification capacity) producing a contextual 0-10 score, and publishes crosswalks to OWASP-Agentic-2026, MAESTRO, AIUC-1, and NIST AI RMF. The kolm engine currently scores severity with a flat critical/high/medium/low rollup and does NOT model autonomy/tool-scope amplification at all (grep for aivss/amplif/autonomy finds only red-team.js and router.js, not the analyzers). Adopting an AIVSS-style amplified severity (without claiming AIUC-1, per hard constraint - align, do not claim) is the single biggest SOTA gap. Competitor/peer posture: most agent-security vendors ship dashboards and runtime guardrails, NOT signed offline-verifiable point-in-time evidence with a transparency log - kolm's signing + Merkle + A/B/C evidence tier is genuinely ahead there. The arxiv 'Auditable Agents' (2604.05485) line of work and zero-trust agent identity frameworks (2505.19301) signal that per-agent identity attestation and decentralized agent auth are emerging buyer expectations the agent-identity analyzer should deepen.
Sources: https://genai.owasp.org/resource/owasp-top-10-for-agentic-applications-for-2026/ , https://cloudsecurityalliance.org/blog/2025/02/06/agentic-ai-threat-modeling-framework-maestro , https://aivss.owasp.org/ , https://aivss.owasp.org/aiuc-aivss-crosswalk

currentState: The security substance behind the ASR report is a deterministic, offline, evidence-disciplined engine plus a consented active battery. PASSIVE (src/red-team.js, RED_TEAM_SPEC_VERSION asr-redteam/0.3): 12 core probes + finance/healthcare domain probes scored over OBSERVED AuditEvents only, with three outcomes (resisted/exposed/untested), a severity-weighted graduated score (SEV_WEIGHT critical=3..info=0.5) computed over EXERCISED probes only, null score when no channel was exercised, and every probe mapped to OWASP LLM/Agentic + MITRE ATLAS. It is rigorous about not fabricating passes (e.g. unicode-homoglyph and credential-in-log return 'untested' rather than 'resisted' when the channel was clean) and stores only opaque event ids, never raw bodies. PERMISSION (src/permission-analyzer.js): granted-vs-used least-privilege diff surfacing wildcard, over-permission, no-declared-grants, undeclared-tool-call, tier-4 high-privilege, sensitive-egress, shared-credential. AUDIT-TRAIL (src/audit-trail-analyzer.js): completeness/tamper-evidence (order-independent hash-chain check), retention, plus a volume-consistency 'curated quiet week' detector (GAP-3). IDENTITY (src/agent-identity-analyzer.js): per (agent,key_id) attestation with graceful 'untested'/'partial' degradation. ACTIVE (src/active-redteam.js, asr-active-redteam/0.1, the +$10,000 Deep Red-Team add-on): a genuine consented offensive battery that REFUSES to send without a consent record naming the endpoint (throws CONSENT_REQUIRED/ENDPOINT_REQUIRED), sends a fixed deterministic corpus to a staging OpenAI-compatible endpoint, and merges via red-team.js mergeActiveResults with worst-wins precedence and per-probe evidence_source labels. CORPUS (src/adversarial-prompts.js, w762-v1): 8 categories x ~6 framing patterns x 5 surface variants (case_flip, unicode_smuggle, base64_wrap, role_flip) with a deterministic mulberry32 PRNG, plus a regex classifier capped at 0.95 confidence. The orchestrator (src/audit-orchestrator.js) folds permission/audit-trail/egress as CORE (ASR-1/2/3), provenance/memory/delegation as SUPPLEMENTAL, and reports injection (ASR-4) separately in red_team rather than inflating the readiness rollup. The exact scope sentence ('Injection is tested and reported, not warranted') is honored across report/audit/spec pages. The deterministic engine is category-strong; the ACTIVE offensive testing layer is shallow (5 probes, 2 single-turn prompts each, one canary-echo classifier, no adaptivity).
sota: 2026 frontier has moved decisively past static corpora toward ADAPTIVE, AGENT-ORCHESTRATED red teaming. OWASP Top 10 for Agentic Applications 2026 (genai.owasp.org) reframes risk as a 'progressive breach model' (compromised intent -> tool/credential power -> cross-agent propagation -> cascading loss of containment) - kolm maps to the older LLM/ASI numbering but does not yet model the cross-agent propagation chain as a single attack path. Methodology SOTA: TAP (Tree of Attacks with Pruning) and PAIR run multi-round attacker-vs-target conversations; PIArena/PISmith (arXiv 2603.13026) use RL + a strategy library to rewrite injections from defense feedback; MUZZLE (arXiv 2602.09222) does adaptive agentic red-teaming of web agents against INDIRECT injection; MonitoringBench (arXiv 2605.09684) red-teams agent monitoring itself. Help Net Security (2026-05-21) reports autonomous red-team agents now solve the majority of black-box challenges, beating human operators on efficiency. Competitor landscape: Gray Swan (CMU spinout, $40M Series A May 2026, trusted by frontier labs) and Haize Labs ('Haizing' automated stress testing) own the active/adaptive testing narrative; kolm's differentiator is NOT attack sophistication but SIGNED, offline-verifiable EVIDENCE. Crucially, on 2026-04-30 CISA/NSA/NCSC/ASD/CCCS/NZ NCSC published joint agentic-AI guidance naming cryptographic attestation + tamper-evident audit trails + comprehensive artifacts as the answer - this is squarely kolm's moat (Ed25519 + SHA-256 Merkle + offline verify) and should be cited as the standard kolm's evidence layer satisfies, while injection sophistication is explicitly out of warranty per the locked scope sentence.

currentState: The signed-evidence report (surface A) is the most mature part of the stack I read. src/attestation-report-builder.js (1922 lines) builds an unsigned envelope from a runAudit() result, canonicalizes it (recursive key-sorted, whitespace-free JSON in canonicalize/canonicalizeReport, lines 161-208), and signs it Ed25519 (signReport, 775-809). The signature covers everything except signature_ed25519, timestamp_evidence, log_checkpoint, co_signatures, and _full_payload - each deliberately detached and documented (205-207). The envelope binds: evidence_digest (sha256 over the analyzed AuditEvents, never the bodies, 80-90), evidence_tier A/B/C with method+basis (coerceEvidenceTier, 350-375), coverage_declaration with a declared-vs-observed window cross-check rendered into signed caveats (696-729), an agent passport, a subprocessor inventory, a red-team block, and a framework crosswalk. Detached evidence is layered after signing: in-line transparency-log inclusion with an embedded RFC 9162 Merkle audit path for fully-offline inclusion verification (recordTransparencyEntry, 111-150), plus an RFC 3161 timestamp via attachDetachedEvidence (843-889). Tiering is real and the paywall is the signature: reduceEnvelopeToScan (533-551) strips report-only sections into a detached, non-signature-covered _full_payload before signing so the scan signature covers exactly the watermarked summary the buyer receives; resignAsTier (922-975) restores it deterministically on purchase without re-running the audit; stripWirePayload (563-569) prevents _full_payload leaking on the wire. tenantHasReportEntitlement (443-465) fails closed. verifyReport (1058-1190) is pure/offline/never-throws and checks schema, signature, signed_at==generated_at, evidence_digest, co-signatures, and issuer-key revocation. Two human renderings exist: renderReportHtml (1215-1595) emits a self-contained WHITE-paper document with a watermark/PREVIEW overlay, verdict band, headline tiles, evidence-tier banner, scope-first caveats, findings, red-team, passport, subprocessor, remediation, and a signature box; renderReportPdf (1626-1919) mirrors it via lazy pdfkit with a frozen no-warm palette. public/report-viewer.html is a separate DARK in-browser lens that fetches/verifies via report-viewer.js and re-scopes tokens to a lit sheet. public/report.html is the marketing field-by-field explainer with a live verify-widget on /sample-audit-report.json; it keeps sig__ok and the verbatim scope sentence. SEPARATELY, src/assurance-case.js + assurance-case-pdf.js implement a model-platform "Trust Packet" (claims/controls/frameworks, v1 + v2 taxonomies) and src/auditor-attestation.js implements the N+7 third-party model-artifact attestation - both are distinct subsystems NOT wired into the ASR report envelope.
sota: 2026 frontier for signed evidence has converged on a few patterns this component is close to but not aligned with. (1) in-toto Attestation Framework / SLSA: the universal envelope is a DSSE wrapper (payloadType + base64 payload + signatures) carrying a typed Statement {_type, subject:[{name,digest}], predicateType, predicate}. kolm invented its own bespoke envelope shape; a typed predicateType + subject.digest array would make kolm reports ingestible by Sigstore/cosign/policy engines (slsa.dev/spec/v1.0/attestation-model, github.com/in-toto/attestation). (2) IETF SCITT (draft-ietf-scitt-architecture-22, expiring Apr 2026) standardizes exactly kolm's model: single-issuer signed statements made transparent via an append-only log that returns a signed receipt - kolm's log_checkpoint is a de-facto SCITT receipt but not in COSE/SCITT format, so it is not interoperable with the emerging standard. (3) SLSA VSA (Verification Summary Attestation) is the canonical "a verifier checked X and it passed/failed" object - directly analogous to kolm's verdict, and a recognized shape buyers' tooling understands. (4) Assurance-case rigor: GSN (Goal/Strategy/Solution/Context, ISO/IEC/SAE structured assurance) and arxiv work on machine-readable safety cases (2403.15236 ACCESS, 1905.02427 SACM) are the academic SOTA; kolm's assurance-case.js is a flat claims+controls list with no argument structure (no strategy/defeater/rebuttal nodes), which reviewers of high-stakes AI increasingly expect. (5) C2PA/Content Credentials popularized embedded-provenance UX (the verify-widget echoes this well). Net: kolm's crypto core meets or exceeds 2026 norms (offline Merkle path embedded, RFC 3161, revocation, co-sign), but the WIRE FORMAT is proprietary where the market standardized on DSSE/in-toto + SCITT receipts.

A signed argument tree for the $25k tier is genuinely novel and on-brand, but it is L effort and MUST keep injection framed as tested-and-reported (not warranted) per the locked scope; constraint-safe only if it does.

verifiedProposals.reason: Real divergence: renderReportHtml ships a white paper sheet while report-viewer.html renders the same JSON on the dark sheet; sharing a template is buildable. Caveat: report.html must keep sig__ok and the site dark backdrop must not change. This is a downloadable report doc (not the site backdrop), so safe, but it is a sizable refactor of two render paths, so plan.

verifiedProposals.title: Enforce co-signer key distinctness in verifyReport (Reviewed Attestation integrity)
verifiedProposals.reason: Confirmed: verifyReport co-signature loop (1148-1162) verifies each block but never checks cv.key_fingerprint !== v.key_fingerprint, while auditor-attestation.js documents same-key=fail as the whole point. Surgical: mark the co-sig untrusted + add one check row. Informational-only so it cannot flip a clean primary verdict. Highest-value safe fix here.

verifiedProposals.title: Carry accreditation + scope on the ASR co-signature block
verifiedProposals.reason: Real: coSig (1024-1033) carries only name/role vs auditor block's richer accreditation/scope. Buildable, BUT the proposal's claim that adding accreditation/scope is 'signature-covered by signing the same canonical payload' is wrong as written: co_signatures is EXCLUDED from canonicalizeReport, so those fields would be unsigned metadata unless the co-sig signs an extended sub-payload. Needs a real signing-shape decision, so plan not ship-now.

verifiedProposals.title: Align the server HTML severity palette with the viewer's no-alarm-red earth tones
verifiedProposals.reason: Confirmed: builder hardcodes #991b1b alarm red (STATUS_COLOR 1203, .finding 1511-1517, PDF bad 1608) while report-viewer.html line 47-49 explicitly chose earth tones (--sev-critical #8C3A2E, --sev-medium #B5852A). Reconciling the builder ramp to the viewer's restrained palette is a surgical token swap, does not touch the site dark backdrop or pricing.html, and removes a real cross-face contradiction.

verifiedProposals.title: Surface the embedded Merkle inclusion path in the rendered signature box, not just the checkpoint summary
verifiedProposals.reason: Confirmed: recordTransparencyEntry embeds inclusion.{leaf_index,audit_path,root_hash} (135-144) but cpLine (1482) and the PDF only print seq/tree_size/root. Adding a one-line 'inclusion proof: embedded (verifiable offline)' affordance surfaces the strongest offline differentiator; small, render-only, signature-neutral.

verifiedProposals.title: Make the report's predicateType/version explicit and stable for future interop
verifiedProposals.reason: Real: AUDIT_REPORT_VERSION is 'asr-report/0.1' (line 60). Bumping is cheap, but it is only worth doing coupled to the in-toto export (whose predicateType is a separate URL anyway); doing it alone changes a signature-covered field on every new report for no immediate buyer value, so sequence it behind invention 1, not ship-now.

verifiedProposals.title: Two divergent visual identities for 'the report' - white-paper HTML vs dark lens
verifiedProposals.reason: Verified accurate: renderReportHtml uses --paper #fff + blue-gradient body (1489-1491) and #991b1b, while report-viewer.html renders the same JSON on the dark sheet with no-alarm-red earth tones (47-49, 90-103). Genuine inconsistency; the palette half is ship-now (proposal 6), the full unified renderer is the plan (proposal 3).

verifiedProposals.title: Report envelope wire format is proprietary, not in-toto/DSSE
verifiedProposals.reason: Verified: buildReportEnvelope (653-687) emits {schema:'kolm-audit-report-1', report_version, summary, findings...} with inline signature_ed25519, no DSSE/predicateType/subject[].digest. Accurately describes the limitation invention 1 addresses; the parallel export unlocks supply-chain tooling without touching the signed bytes.

verifiedProposals.title: log_checkpoint is a de-facto transparency receipt but not in SCITT/COSE format
verifiedProposals.reason: Verified: recordTransparencyEntry returns {origin,tree_size,root_hash,leaf_hash,seq,inclusion:{audit_path}} (118-146) in a kolm-private JSON shape, functionally a SCITT-style receipt but not COSE_Sign1. Accurate gap, but COSE encoding likely needs a CBOR/COSE dep (must verify none exists) and SCITT is still draft; lower priority interop, plan only.

verifiedProposals.title: assurance-case.js is structurally weak vs GSN/SACM and disconnected from the ASR report
verifiedProposals.reason: Verified: buildAssuranceCase (437-485) emits flat claims[]+controls[] over model-platform artifacts (manifest/receipt), no strategy/defeater nodes, and the ASR envelope carries only buildCaveats (313-333) - no formal argument. Accurately frames where invention 2 belongs; same scope constraint on injection applies.

verifiedProposals.title: Two unrelated co-signature mechanisms invite confusion and key-policy gaps
verifiedProposals.reason: Verified: addCoSignature/loadCoSignerFromEnv (999-1052, KOLM_COSIGNER_PRIVATE_KEY) carries only name/role and verifyReport does NOT enforce distinct keys, while auditor-attestation.js (KOLM_AUDITOR_ED25519_PRIVATE_KEY) has auditor_id/accreditation/scope/cross-check and header 65-70 makes same-key a hard fail. Accurate; the distinctness half is ship-now (proposal 4), reusing the richer block semantics is the plan.

verifiedProposals.title: Co-signer key distinctness not enforced in verifyReport for the Reviewed Attestation tier
verifiedProposals.reason: Verified duplicate of proposal 4's finding and equally real: verifyReport (1146-1163) never compares cs.key_fingerprint to the issuer fingerprint, so an issuer==cosigner misconfig would still render 'co-signed', defeating the tier premise that auditor-attestation.js already guards. Same surgical fix; ship the check now.

currentState: The crypto moat is genuinely strong and broad, but split across two generations of code with one live regression. SOLID CORE: src/ed25519.js is clean (Node crypto.sign(null), PEM/SPKI, 32-hex SHA256 fingerprint, RFC 8037 OKP JWK export via publicKeyJwk, env+disk+generated loader). src/merkle.js is a correct, dependency-free RFC 6962/9162 implementation (leafHash 0x00, nodeHash 0x01, MTH, inclusionPath, verifyInclusion with the proper fn/sn/LSB walk and a length-checked timingSafeEqual root compare). src/transparency-log.js builds on it: hash-chain (prev_hash||body) AND Merkle tree head, C2SP signed-note checkpoints (signTreeHead), optional witness cosigning (cosignTreeHead/verifyCosignedTreeHead via KOLM_TLOG_WITNESS_KEY), persisted checkpoint history, and verifyTransparencyAppend. transparency-anchor.js does the right batching design (client-side Merkle batch, one anchor per batch, off-hot-path ReceiptAnchorBatcher, two-level offline verifyReceiptAnchor) and degrades to state:'local' without throwing. transparency-log-routes.js exposes a no-account public GET surface (size/entries/proof/checkpoints) with paging caps. pubkey-directory.js gates registration with a sign-the-nonce proof-of-control challenge (issueChallenge/registerKey) and corrupt-file recovery. Revocation is correctly wired into the PURE verifier: attestation-report-builder.js:1174 consults key-revocation.js status() and returns issuer_key_revoked everywhere verifyReport runs (route, CLI, SDK), with rotation!=compromise semantics. ensure-signing-key.js guarantees a persisted volume key on boot (fixing the historical signing_key:missing). cid.js is a deterministic content-id deliberately independent of signing key. WEAK SPOTS (grounded): (1) sigstore.js:400-477 still hand-rolls its OWN RFC 6962 inclusion verifier - the exact code merkle.js was written to replace - and it never imports merkle.js; its root compare Buffer.alloc(32,0).fill(computed.slice(0,32)) truncates roots to 32 bytes and its lone-right-edge branch sets cursor=sib then continues without consuming, and this verifier is LIVE on the report path via binder.js verifySigstoreBundle. (2) There is NO consistency proof (RFC 9162 2.1.4) anywhere - merkle.js has zero consistency support - so the witness/split-view story that the comments promise cannot actually be enforced. (3) Two unlinked key-lifecycle modules: keys.js (CLI rotate/KMS-intent, on-disk keys-state.json) and key-revocation.js (SQLite issuer_key_status consulted by the verifier) never call each other; a kolm keys rotate does NOT mark the old key rotated in the verifier store nor register the new pubkey. (4) keys.js emits honest_scope strings in its rotation/export manifest, surfaced verbatim by cli/kolm.js keys rotate --json (a customer/API surface).
sota: 2026 frontier for transparency-log trust: C2SP has standardized the signed-checkpoint note format (tlog-checkpoint.md), a synchronous HTTP witness protocol (tlog-witness.md) and cosignature format (tlog-cosignature.md) where a witness only cosigns AFTER verifying a CONSISTENCY proof against the prior tree head it signed - that consistency check is the actual mechanism that defeats split-view, and kolm's witness code lacks it. C2SP now recommends ML-DSA-44 (post-quantum) for checkpoint signatures; kolm is Ed25519-only (acceptable, but the constraint forbids changing the trust path to anything but Ed25519+SHA-256, so PQ is a documented roadmap item, not a swap). Sigstore Rekor v2 went GA Oct 2025 on a tile-based log (Trillian-Tessera), distributing the trust root + per-shard log public keys via TUF and looking up keys by logID = SHA256(log pubkey); kolm's sigstore.js still targets Rekor v1 /api/v1/log/entries hashedrekord and does not pin logID to a trusted-root key. kolm's append-only-witness + offline-inclusion + Ed25519-pinned-checkpoint design is squarely on the CT/Sigstore-witness model and competitive; the gaps are consistency proofs, a single source of truth for key state, and retiring the legacy v1 Merkle verifier. Sources: datatracker.ietf.org/doc/rfc9162, github.com/C2SP/C2SP (tlog-checkpoint.md, tlog-witness.md, tlog-cosignature.md), blog.sigstore.dev/rekor-v2-ga, github.com/sigstore/rekor-tiles.

verifiedProposals.title: Consistency-proof + cosigning witness loop that actually proves append-only
verifiedProposals.reason: Verified: merkle.js has computeRoot/inclusionPath/verifyInclusion but ZERO consistency support (grep returns 0); cosignTreeHead signs blind and the checkpoints history (routes:233-246) advertises detecting non-append-only rewrites it cannot prove. RFC 9162 2.1.4 is pure SHA-256, zero deps, ports to SDKs. Closes the core append-only claim. Sized M but is the highest-value crypto gap; plan rather than ship-now because the witness-guard wiring + route + SDK port is a real multi-file build, not surgical.

verifiedProposals.title: Single signing-key state machine: rotation, revocation, directory, checkpoint read one ledger
verifiedProposals.reason: Real divergence confirmed: keys.js writes on-disk keys-state.json while key-revocation.js owns the SQLite issuer_key_status table the verifier actually reads, and keys.js explicitly does NOT register the new pubkey (comment lines 30-32). Unifying them and anchoring key-lifecycle events into the same tlog is sound and on-brand. Effort L and it touches CLI + audit-routes + three stores; correct as plan. Note buildSketch references pubkey-directory.registerKey via an internal self-signed challenge — workable since issueChallenge/registerKey exist in-process, no network.

verifiedProposals.title: Offline key-validity proof bound to report signing time
verifiedProposals.reason: Depends entirely on the key-ledger invention above (key-lifecycle events must exist in the tlog first), so it cannot ship independently. The pattern (non-signed side block like anchor/latency_breakdown, verified via verifyInclusionProof against a pinned checkpoint) is consistent with the existing report builder design and genuinely upgrades the offline trust story the $750/$25k tiers sell. Plan, gated behind invention #2.

verifiedProposals.title: Delete sigstore.js home-grown Merkle verifier; delegate to merkle.js
verifiedProposals.reason: Verified against source: sigstore.js:466-469 wraps both roots in Buffer.alloc(32,0).fill(...slice(0,32)) (truncates/zero-pads non-32B roots, compares only 32B) and the lone-right-edge branch (442-451) does cursor=sib;continue without correct sibling handling. merkle.js:7-12 was written explicitly to replace this and is reachable from binder.js:372 verifySigstoreBundle (the report verifier). Surgical S-effort swap to merkle.verifyInclusion using the shared leafHash. A false-accepting verifier on the report path is the single most direct moat risk; ship now.

verifiedProposals.title: Rename honest_scope to caveat/scope on the keys.js manifests
verifiedProposals.reason: Confirmed hard-constraint violation: keys.js:208 and :239 set honest_scope, and cli/kolm.js:16032-16035 builds receipt={...manifest}, deletes ONLY private_key_pem, then prints the whole object via JSON.stringify on `kolm keys rotate --json` (a customer/API surface). The forbidden word 'honest' is live in stdout today. Pure string-key rename (scope_note) + update tests/wave193-keys-rotate.test.js. Trivial and mandatory.

verifiedProposals.title: Serve the public log key as JWKS at a discovery route
verifiedProposals.reason: Verified: ed25519.js:255 publicKeyJwk produces a conformant RFC 8037 OKP JWK and its comment (250-253) literally promises /.well-known/jwks.json, but no route serves it. loadOrCreateDefaultSigner is already imported in transparency-log-routes.js. Adding GET /v1/transparency-log/jwks.json is a few lines, no new deps, only already-public key material. Must also add the path to PUBLIC_API in auth.js (the routes file itself notes it does not own auth). Small, real, useful for JOSE verifiers of the jws-envelope representation.

verifiedProposals.title: Surface witnessed boolean + witness key_ids on /proof and /checkpoints
verifiedProposals.reason: Accurate: currentSignedHead (66-87) cosigns (no-op without witness) and captureCheckpoint persists best-effort, but responses never surface whether a witness attested. Adding a witnessed flag + witness key_ids derived from checkpoint.witnesses is a small read-only response enrichment. Useful but lower value until consistency proofs (proposal #1) make the witness load-bearing; still a safe quick win and good documentation of the KOLM_TLOG_WITNESS_KEY operator step.

verifiedProposals.title: Pin Rekor logID to a configured trusted key/allowlist before accepting v1 inclusion
verifiedProposals.reason: Verified: sigstore.js:348-349 deliberately does not enforce a trust root for logID, so a self-consistent proof from an attacker-run Rekor is accepted. An optional KOLM_SIGSTORE_REKOR_LOG_PUBKEY / logID allowlist is a clean, opt-in, offline-safe hardening that does not touch the Ed25519 trust path. Lower priority than fixing the broken verifier (#4) since sigstore is the secondary public-transparency layer, not the primary moat; plan.

verifiedProposals.title: GAP: Live broken RFC 6962 inclusion verifier in sigstore.js on the report path
verifiedProposals.reason: Confirmed at source: the truncating Buffer.alloc(32,0).fill root compare (466-469) and the broken lone-right-edge branch (442-451) are real, and verifySigstoreBundle is reached from binder.js:372 (the report/receipt verifier). merkle.js:7-12 documents it exists to replace exactly this walk. This is the gap that proposal #4 fixes; same finding, accurately stated. Highest-urgency correctness gap.

verifiedProposals.title: GAP: No consistency proof - witness/split-view claim is unenforceable
verifiedProposals.reason: Verified: grep for 'consistency' in merkle.js returns 0; transparency-log.js:453-462 and routes:233-246 advertise detecting non-append-only rewrites / split view with no primitive to prove tree_head(n) is a prefix of tree_head(m); cosignTreeHead signs without verifying consistency. Accurate gap; the fix is proposal #1. Plan.

verifiedProposals.title: GAP: keys.js leaks forbidden word 'honest' to a customer/API surface
verifiedProposals.reason: Confirmed end-to-end: keys.js:208/:239 honest_scope reaches stdout via cli/kolm.js:16032-16035 (--json prints receipt={...manifest} minus only private_key_pem). Direct hard-constraint violation, live today. Same finding as improvement #5; ship the rename now.

verifiedProposals.title: GAP: Two disjoint, unsynchronized key-lifecycle stores
verifiedProposals.reason: Verified: keys.js maintains keys-state.json (rotateKey/listKeys/activeFingerprint) while key-revocation.js owns issuer_key_status (the table the verifier consults), and keys.js comment 30-32 confirms it does NOT register the new pubkey. `kolm keys rotate` does not mark the old fp rotated in issuer_key_status nor register the new key, so the verifier still treats it default-live and the new key is undiscoverable. Accurate operational gap; fix is invention #2. Plan.

verifiedProposals.title: GAP: sigstore.js targets Rekor v1 only; no logID->trusted-key pinning
verifiedProposals.reason: Code-accurate: sigstore.js hits /api/v1/log/entries with hashedrekord 0.0.1 (Rekor v1) and 348-349 does not enforce the logID trust root. The Rekor v2 GA Oct 2025 / tile-based + TUF detail is plausible and within knowledge cutoff but is a forward-looking claim, not verifiable from this repo. The actionable, in-repo half (logID pinning) is captured by improvement #8; full v2 migration is a larger speculative effort. Plan, lower priority.

verifiedProposals.title: GAP: No JWKS / pubkey discovery route despite publicKeyJwk implemented
verifiedProposals.reason: Verified: ed25519.js:255 publicKeyJwk + the /.well-known/jwks.json comment exist, jws-envelope publicJwk exists, but no route in transparency-log-routes.js or pubkey-directory serves it. Accurate gap; fix is improvement #6. Small and safe to ship (remember auth.js PUBLIC_API allowlist).

currentState: The verify-layer is the strongest-built part of the trust moat and is close to category-best. Offline browser verification (public/kolm-audit-verify.js) does REAL WebCrypto Ed25519 (RFC 8037), with canonicalization explicitly documented as byte-identical to the Node builder (recursive key-sorted whitespace-free JSON; signature_ed25519/timestamp_evidence/log_checkpoint/co_signatures/_full_payload excluded, copied via object-rest to match the server's spread even for hostile __proto__ keys). verifyAuditReport runs a genuine check sequence: schema, signature-block shape, independent fingerprint recompute + cross-check of the claimed key_fingerprint, optional pinning, the Ed25519 verify, signed_at-vs-generated_at consistency (catches post-sign timestamp edits), and informational surfacing of timestamp_evidence/evidence_digest/passport. Tier-3 revocation is checked BEFORE the WebCrypto availability gate so a revoked key is refused even on a browser lacking native Ed25519. verifyInclusionOffline implements the RFC 9162 inclusion walk (0x00/0x01 domain separation) over the EMBEDDED log_checkpoint.inclusion path, fully offline, with cross-checks that inclusion.root_hash/tree_size match the checkpoint. public/verify.html is a polished three-tier verifier (signature / issuer keyring / revocation) with self-demonstrating Tamper and Forge-with-rogue-key buttons (mints a real in-browser Ed25519 key, re-signs edited bytes, shows tier-1 pass + tier-2 fail), inlined offline keyring anchor merged with a best-effort fetch of /keys/kolm-issuers.json, calm-on-unreachable live revocation/log lookups, and one-click /verify?trust=<slug> auto-load that forces the strict issuer pin. The server mirror POST /v1/audit/report/verify (PUBLIC, in PUBLIC_API, per-IP rate-limited 120/min, never-throws) returns {trusted, verify, issuer, revocation} where trusted requires verify.ok AND issuer.recognized AND not-revoked, with _issuerProvenance matching live signer or published keyring and _embeddedKeyFingerprint recomputed (never trusting the claimed fp). GET /v1/trust/:slug serves the paid signed report (html/json/pdf), strips _full_payload defensively, injects a reviewer toolbar + freshness banner, and links to /verify?trust=. Auth allow-listing in src/auth.js is careful and well-commented (verify, issuer-key, issuer-key/:fp/status, transparency-log read paths, trust slug + export/delta/badge/questionnaire/unlock, with the seller-side analytics deliberately left auth-gated).
sota: 2026 frontier for offline artifact verification is Sigstore/cosign v3 bundles: the entire trust context (signature, cert chain, RFC 3161 signed timestamp, and a Signed Entry Timestamp [SET] proving log inclusion) ships in ONE file and verifies fully offline, including the log's own signed promise of inclusion - no live call to the log. NVIDIA NGC model signing (Jul 2025), PyPI (Nov 2024), Maven Central (Jan 2025) all rely on offline verify, TUF-delivered key material, and tile-based transparency logs. kolm already matches the hard parts (Ed25519, byte-stable canonicalization, embedded RFC 9162 inclusion proof, published keyring). The two SOTA deltas: (1) kolm has NO offline-verifiable log PROMISE equivalent to a SET - the embedded inclusion proof proves the leaf is in a tree with a given root, but the root itself is not signed by the log in a way the browser checks offline, and revocation/freshness still need a live fetch (treated, correctly, as calm not fatal). (2) No consistency-proof or witness-cosign in the browser trust path, and co_signatures (named-reviewer attestations, the $25k Reviewed Attestation tier) are EXCLUDED from canonical bytes but never verified client-side, so the marquee tier has no offline proof surface. Sources: https://docs.sigstore.dev/about/bundle/ , https://blog.sigstore.dev/cosign-3-0-available/ , https://docs.sigstore.dev/cosign/verifying/verify/

verifiedProposals.title: Self-contained offline evidence bundle with a log-signed checkpoint (kolm SET)
verifiedProposals.reason: Confirmed gap: recordTransparencyEntry (attestation-report-builder.js:118-128) embeds root_hash/root_b64 but NO signature, and verifyInclusionOffline (kolm-audit-verify.js:241-300) anchors to that self-supplied, attacker-controllable root. The premise is slightly overstated - src/transparency-log.js:268 already has signTreeHead (C2SP Ed25519 note) + a verifier - so this is wiring the existing signed head into log_checkpoint.signed_head + a pinned log key in the keyring, not net-new crypto. High-value, Ed25519+SHA-256 only (no blockchain), respects no-new-deps. Larger than a one-liner: keyring schema + browser verify path + server embed; plan, not ship-now.

verifiedProposals.title: Browser co-signature verification panel for the Reviewed Attestation tier
verifiedProposals.reason: Accurate: canonicalizeReport (kolm-audit-verify.js:81-82) destructures and EXCLUDES co_signatures expressly so they verify over the stable payload, yet nothing iterates or renders them, and kolm-issuers.json has no reviewers[] (grep empty). The $25k named-co-signer tier has zero browser proof surface today. Buildable with existing WebCrypto/canonicalizer; surfaces an existing tier so no pricing change (locked pricing safe). Ties surface A's marquee tier to its moat. Requires real co-signer keys provisioned before it shows anything truthful, so plan, not ship-now.

verifiedProposals.title: One-line copy-paste offline verifier (curl | node) printed inside every report
verifiedProposals.reason: Real persona fit (skeptical buyer who will not run vendor JS) and buildable with node:crypto only, no new deps/CDNs, ASCII-safe. Risk: a second canonicalizer implementation must stay byte-identical to kolm-audit-verify.js forever or it becomes a false-negative generator - real maintenance hazard. Pinning a SHA-256 of verify.mjs inside the report is sound. Medium value, medium effort; plan and treat canonical-byte parity as a test-enforced contract.

verifiedProposals.title: Make the remote keyring fetch additive-only for pinned kids
verifiedProposals.reason: Verified bug: refreshKeyring (verify.html:516-517) does byKid.set(i.kid, i) which OVERWRITES the inlined anchor's public_key for kolm-prod-2026, directly contradicting the file's own comment 'the inlined copy is the offline anchor, not the fetch' (lines 483-484). A cache-poisoned/edge-stale keyring response defeats the pin. Surgical fix (keep inlined public_key on kid conflict, only ADD new kids / merge revocations), small, no constraint impact, high security value. Same-TLS-origin lowers exploitability but the pin promise is load-bearing brand copy.

verifiedProposals.title: Bring the HTTP verify endpoint to parity with the browser verdict
verifiedProposals.reason: Premise is FALSE as written. verifyReport (attestation-report-builder.js:1093-1098) ALREADY performs the exact signed_at != generated_at check and returns ok:false with the same reason string as the browser. The route also already adds issuer + revocation (audit-routes.js:1296) and exposes trusted. The only residual delta is that the HTTP response does not surface the offline-inclusion line - cosmetic/informational, never flips a verdict. Not the parity gap claimed; reject.

verifiedProposals.title: Surface a clear 'verify offline yourself' affordance pointing at the open source
verifiedProposals.reason: Grounded: the band already links kolm-audit-verify.js + keyring (verify.html:234-235) only in the footer band; a verdict-adjacent 'view the verifier source / these are the keys this page trusts' callout is a reasonable copy/affordance nudge. Inline graphics/text only, dark backdrop untouched, no constraint risk. Low impact, low effort - a content tweak, not a moat move; plan/backlog.

verifiedProposals.title: Render the live-revocation 'unreachable' state more decisively for trust-link reviewers
verifiedProposals.reason: Accurate: on the strict ?trust= path, the unreachable branch (verify.html:672-673) already says the published-keyring anchor was used, but the headline could still read as 'revocation not checked'. Adding one clarifying line that the offline revocation list WAS consulted is a small, truthful copy fix that prevents a false 'silently revocable' impression for the exact reviewer persona. Note: the existing line already mostly does this, so scope is a wording tightening - low risk, ship-now.

verifiedProposals.title: Tighten the trust-slug validation comment/regex symmetry
verifiedProposals.reason: Verified mismatch: client checks /^[A-Za-z0-9_-]{6,80}$/ (verify.html:908) while every server trust route is {1,64} (auth.js:565,572,578,583). A 65-80 char slug passes the client then 404s server-side; a 1-5 char slug is server-acceptable but client-rejected. Real edge bug. Slugs are crypto.randomBytes so live links are unaffected today, capping impact, but aligning bounds to {1,64} is a trivial, safe correctness fix; ship-now.

verifiedProposals.title: Signed log checkpoint (SET-equivalent) is not verified offline; offline inclusion anchors to an UNSIGNED root
verifiedProposals.reason: Confirmed in code: verifyInclusionOffline (kolm-audit-verify.js:252,296-300) recomputes the root and compares it to inc.root_hash/checkpoint.root_hash - both self-supplied by the report - with no signature over that root. A forger controlling the bytes can embed a self-consistent leaf/path/root and the offline inclusion line passes; only the live /size fetch (calm-on-unreachable) would diverge. Real P1 in the trust path. (Minor inaccuracy: server-side signTreeHead exists, so the fix is wiring, not inventing.) Pairs with invention #1; plan.

verifiedProposals.title: Co-signatures (the $25,000 Reviewed Attestation tier) have no offline verification surface
verifiedProposals.reason: Verified: canonicalizeReport excludes co_signatures (kolm-audit-verify.js:81-82) precisely so they verify over the stable payload, but verifyAuditReport never iterates report.co_signatures and verify.html never renders a co-signed line; keyring has no reviewers[]. The premium tier's entire value (a named co-signer) is unprovable in the buyer's browser. Real, high-value gap; pairs with invention #2. Plan.

verifiedProposals.title: Server verify endpoint omits the signed_at-vs-generated_at and inclusion checks the browser performs
verifiedProposals.reason: FALSE. verifyReport (attestation-report-builder.js:1093-1098) performs the identical signed_at != generated_at check and fails with the same reason; the route adds issuer + revocation and exposes trusted (audit-routes.js:1279-1297). A CLI/SDK consumer checking trusted gets the same verdict as a human - the two paths are equivalent on every verdict-flipping check. Only the informational offline-inclusion line is not echoed (never changes the verdict). Hallucinated gap; reject.

verifiedProposals.title: Issuer keyring fetch merges remote entries by kid with no integrity binding
verifiedProposals.reason: Verified: refreshKeyring (verify.html:516-517) byKid.set overwrites the inlined anchor's public_key for a matching kid (e.g. kolm-prod-2026), defeating the documented 'inlined copy is the offline anchor' pin (lines 483-484) against a cache-poisoned same-origin response. Same finding as improvement #4 - the fix (anchor wins on kid conflict; remote ADD-only + revocation-merge) is small and safe; ship-now. Counts once with #4.

verifiedProposals.title: No log consistency proof or split-view detection in the trust path
verifiedProposals.reason: Accurate: the codebase has Merkle inclusion but no consistency-proof verifier in the trust path (no verifyConsistency over checkpoints; PUBLIC_API allow-lists transparency-log size|entries|proof|checkpoints, auth.js:557, but no consistency endpoint). Append-only / non-equivocation cannot be shown to a reviewer today. currentState: The paid loop is real and well-architected for the happy path, but has structural revenue-leak and orphaned-sweep gaps. Checkout: src/asr-billing.js exposes a pure ref-encode/decode catalog (report $750, starter $299, growth $999, full $15k, plus $3.5k, reviewed $25k, redteam $10k) and fails loud with BillingNotConfiguredError (503) when unconfigured. Two checkout paths exist: Checkout Sessions API (secret key + price id) and hosted Payment Links (link env + webhook secret only), with client_reference_id prefix encoding (asrrep_/asrsub_/asrpkg_/asrent_) because Payment Links cannot carry metadata. Webhook (src/router.js 13133-13492): HMAC-SHA256 sig verify with constant-time compare, stripe_events dedup inside withTransaction (BEGIN IMMEDIATE), branches on metadata.kolm_product or ref prefix (never amount_total), with tenant-fencing + post-write read-back returning {retryable} so Stripe re-delivers on unconfirmed writes. Fulfillment (src/asr-fulfillment.js) rebuilds the full report from stored logs to avoid the signed-but-empty trap, has A13 dup-active guard on activateSubscription, and idempotent fulfillReportPurchase. Dunning (src/dunning.js): 3/7/14-day retry ladder, idempotent scheduleDunning, pure tEmailDunning template, advanceOne atomic claim. Invoices (src/invoices.js): idempotent recordInvoiceFromStripe, recordRefund writes a credit-memo only. Pro-rata downgrade credit math exists in src/stripe.js computeProrataCredit/prorataCreditFromEvent. CRITICAL DEFECTS confirmed by grep: runDueDunning (dunning.js:123) has ZERO call sites; resignPendingReports has ZERO call sites; the only scheduler tick (src/audit-routes.js:1664, KOLM_CRON_SECRET-gated) calls runDueReattestations only. No charge.dispute.created/closed or charge.refunded webhook case exists anywhere. advanceOne (dunning.js:104-117) suspends only the dunning row, never calls setSubscriptionStatus. src/chargeback.js is a naming collision: it is LLM cost-attribution (w783-v1), not payment-dispute handling.
sota: 2026 SaaS billing baselines this loop is measured against: (1) Stripe webhook idempotency via event-id dedup + handler-level idempotency keys is now table stakes; this codebase does it correctly with stripe_events dedup in a serialized txn. (2) Dispute/refund auto-revocation: the 2026 norm for entitlement systems is to revoke access on charge.dispute.created and charge.refunded, not just on subscription cancel; Stripe Radar Early Fraud Warnings (radar.early_fraud_warning.created) are the recommended pre-dispute freeze signal to avoid the 0.75% dispute-rate threshold that triggers monitoring programs. This loop handles NONE of these. (3) Dunning: Stripe Smart Retries + decline-code-aware ladders (insufficient_funds retries late-month near payday; expired_card stops retrying and prompts update) are standard; this loop uses a flat 3/7/14 ladder ignoring decline_code. (4) Revenue reconciliation: signed/auditable daily reconciliation between Stripe charges and internal entitlements is the recommended defense against silently-dropped webhooks (webhook delivery is at-least-once but not guaranteed); this loop has no reconciliation sweep, so a lost checkout.session.completed = paid-but-unfulfilled with no recovery. (5) For a trust/attestation product specifically, the differentiating 2026 move is making revocation itself cryptographically transparent (Merkle-logged status change) so a refunded report cannot keep verifying as valid - this aligns with the existing Ed25519 + RFC-6962 transparency moat.

currentState: Auth is an API-key model with credible hygiene but no true session layer. src/auth.js: keys minted as `ks_`/`kao_` + 16 random bytes; stored only as `sha256:` hash (api_key_hash) with a 10-char prefix; constant-time compare via timingSafeEqual; one-shot migration clears any legacy plaintext column at module load (migrateAllPlainKeysOnce). Resolution is findTenantByApiKey -> a LINEAR scan over all tenants (findOne predicate hashes the candidate per row), with a fallback linear scan over a separate `api_keys` table for W936 scoped keys. authMiddleware enforces precedence cookie > Authorization Bearer > X-API-Key, rejects ?api_key= query (401, loud), supports an ADMIN_KEY bypass tenant, anon-token expiry, per-tenant token-bucket rate limit (in-memory Map, lost on restart / not shared across instances), and monthly quota headers. Scoped member keys (mintScopedKey) carry scopes[] + optional TTL (M15) and keyHasScope() gating. Tenant isolation is NOT enforced by a central layer: req.tenant is set to the tenant NAME (not id) for primary-key auth but to the tenant ID on the soft-auth public path and in account-ui-routes _tenantIdOf — an inconsistency. Isolation depends entirely on every store predicate carrying a tenant filter (e.g. account-ui-routes scopes memStore/event reads by rec.tenant_id === tenant; SCIM handlers pass tenant.id into scimProvisioning). The 'web session' is the raw API key placed verbatim into the httpOnly kolm_session cookie (router.js signin/signup/session-login, oauth.js, saml-acs) with secure:isProductionRuntime(), sameSite:'lax', fixed maxAge 30d, sliding-refreshed on every /v1/account hit. There is NO opaque session-id indirection and NO server-side session record/revocation — logout only clears the browser cookie; the underlying key stays valid. NOTE: src/sessions.js is unrelated to web auth — it is a CLI detached-process/log-tail manager (detach/resume/rescue over jobs.jsonl). PUBLIC_API is a ~170-clause allowlist function (giant boolean OR of literals + regexes); soft-auth populates req.tenant_record on public routes if a valid key is present. OAuth (google/github) uses a random state cookie checked on callback but no PKCE and no OIDC nonce; redirect_uri is server-built (good) and safeReturn blocks open-redirect (must start with single '/'). SCIM bearer tokens stored hashed; _scimResolveTenant compares with a plain === on the hash (not constant-time).
sota: 2026 baselines for this domain: (1) Secret-scanning-friendly key design — distinct, registrable prefixes per key class + a checksum so GitHub/GitGuardian secret-scanning partner program can auto-detect and the server can reject malformed keys with zero DB hit (Zuplo, DevCycle, GitHub partner program). kolm has prefixes (ks_/kao_) but NO checksum and NO partner registration. (2) Cookie hardening — OWASP CSRF + 2026 cookie guides recommend Secure + HttpOnly + SameSite=Strict for auth cookies plus a double-submit/synchronizer CSRF token; kolm uses SameSite=Lax with no CSRF token (own audit B5 flags this). (3) Session best practice (OWASP Authentication/Session cheat sheets) — opaque session identifier distinct from the long-lived credential, server-side session store, regeneration on login, idle + absolute timeout, and real server-side revocation on logout; kolm stores the raw bearer key in the cookie and has none of these (own audit B8). (4) Bearer-token guidance (OWASP OAuth2) — audience-restricted, short-lived, rotation; API keys long-lived must be rotatable + leak-revocable (kolm has rotateTenantKey + scoped TTL, good). (5) OAuth — RFC 9700 / OAuth 2.1 expect PKCE on all auth-code flows and OIDC nonce when ID-token claims are consumed; kolm omits both. (6) Multi-tenant isolation (AWS SaaS guidance) — isolation should be enforced at a central choke point (row-level policy / scoped data accessor), not re-implemented per query; kolm relies on per-predicate discipline, which is fragile.

currentState:
Two parallel persistence layers, both real and defended. (1) src/store.js: JSON-file driver (dev) + node:sqlite driver (prod, auto on RAILWAY_ENVIRONMENT/NODE_ENV=production). SQLite: WAL + synchronous=FULL + busy_timeout=30000 + foreign_keys=ON, expression indexes on json_extract($.tenant_id/$.id/$.public_slug/$.stripe_subscription_id). JSON driver: temp+fsync+atomic-rename, dir fsync, Windows EPERM/EBUSY retry+copy fallback, .bak mirror, corrupt-file quarantine + backup recovery. Re-entrant txns via BEGIN IMMEDIATE/SAVEPOINT. Fail-closed boot guard refuses production start if KOLM_DATA_DIR unwritable (lines 41-59). staged_captures quarantine (W808) here. (2) event-store.js: separate node:sqlite at ~/.kolm/events/events.sqlite, JSONL fallback with read-time dedupe, additive ALTER migration, self-healing EACCES fallback. (3) store-backup.js: sync never-throws snapshotter; SQLite VACUUM INTO, JSON copies *.json; pruneBackups(keep=14); wired in server.js every 6h + startup +2min + SIGTERM. (4) audit-retention.js: per-tenant [90..2555]d, default 365, dry-run default, live eviction needs confirm:true AND dry_run:false, tenant-fenced, delegates to event-store purgeEvents. (5) artifact-lifecycle.js + evidence-store.js: per-artifact JSON files on volume, atomic temp+rename, validated state machine, canPull() block on revoked. (6) store-drivers/ has vercel-postgres.js + vercel-kv.js but store.js only wires json|sqlite. transparency-log.js (RFC 6962/9162 + Ed25519 C2SP checkpoints) is the moat but NOT connected to backup/persistence.</currentState>

sota: SOTA for single-instance SQLite-on-volume in 2026: WAL + synchronous=FULL is the correct baseline (code matches). DR gap-closer is off-host streaming replication via Litestream (WAL pages to S3, point-in-time recovery, survives volume loss). VACUUM INTO is a same-host consistent snapshot, not DR. Best practice requires integrity_check on each snapshot plus a periodic restore drill - neither exists here. Differentiator for an evidence product: RFC 9162 Merkle consistency proofs prove append-only across two tree heads (~3KB for 80M events), enabling backup/restore to prove no signed evidence was dropped. merkle.js ships inclusion proofs only. Refs: rfc-editor.org/info/rfc9162, datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc6962, oldmoe.blog/2024/04/30 backup-strategies-for-sqlite.

currentState: Solid, deliberately-hand-rolled prod hardening for a single long-lived Railway web instance. server.js wires: helmet with a real CSP (default-src 'self', frame-ancestors none, object-src none, HSTS 2y+preload, nosniff, referrer strict-origin-when-cross-origin), compression that excludes SSE, raw-body only for /v1/stripe/webhook then express.json (4mb), trust proxy:2 in prod, a standardized 400/413/500 error handler emitting an opaque error_id + X-Kolm-Error-Id and Sentry capture, branded 404/500 pages, process guards (uncaughtException -> graceful drain, unhandledRejection -> log+report, SIGTERM/SIGINT -> backup snapshot + drain), and an E6 production fail-fast: runtimeReadiness() blocks boot when required checks fail. env.js centralizes isProductionRuntime(), runtimeReadiness() (receipt_secret/store_driver/data_dir/artifact_dir required in prod; auto-bootstraps writable tmp dirs so /ready is green out of the box), and typed readers envBool/envSecret that fix the !!process.env truthiness and ''-as-configured bugs. env-normalize.js maps operator env-var casings/aliases to canonical UPPER_SNAKE at boot. otel.js is a 1000+-line hand-rolled OTLP/HTTP exporter (no SDK dep) with W733 kolm.* attrs, W921 OTel GenAI semconv v1.37 (gen_ai.* spans+histograms with exact bucket bounds), tenant-id hashing for privacy, content capture opt-in/post-redaction only; gated by KOLM_OTEL=1, honest no-op otherwise. sentry-init.js is an opt-in dynamic-import shim (no dep in package.json). cloudflare.js generates (but the app does not itself enforce) edge WAF + rate-limit + email-routing rules idempotently. Rate limiting is express-rate-limit with ~18 named limiters keyed by coalesced ipKey (IPv4 /24, IPv6 /48) or tenantOrIpKey, all using the DEFAULT in-memory MemoryStore. /health is rich (git sha, region, uptime, capture_store, signing_key, storage writability probe, store_ephemeral, backend). /ready returns 200/503 from runtimeReadiness. ensure-signing-key.js generates+persists an Ed25519 key under KOLM_DATA_DIR/keys on boot so the trust moat never ships unsigned.
sota: 2026 strict-CSP guidance (web.dev/articles/strict-csp, MDN CSP impl guide) is unambiguous: allowing all inline scripts via 'unsafe-inline' is the canonical XSS risk; for static pages the recommended path is hash-based script-src (sha256/384/512, base64) which lets you drop 'unsafe-inline' without per-request nonce infra; modern browsers ignore 'unsafe-inline' once a hash/nonce is present, so adding hashes is safely incremental. express-rate-limit docs + 2026 production write-ups (dev.to axiom_agent; oneuptime redis-express-rate-limiting) state the default MemoryStore is per-process and reset on restart, so security/abuse limits (signin/signup) silently reset on every Railway deploy and would 3x under any horizontal scale; Redis-backed stores are the accepted production answer when accuracy matters (security limits, paid quotas). OTel GenAI SemConv reached v1.37 (gen_ai.provider.name replacing deprecated gen_ai.system); the code already tracks this, which is genuinely ahead of most peers. Peer agent-security/evidence vendors increasingly publish a machine-readable /.well-known/security.txt and a signed build-provenance/SLSA-style attestation of the running server image - kolm signs artifacts but not its own deployment.

verifiedProposals.title: Hash-pinned strict CSP generated at build from the static public/*.html set

Buildable but M: every inline <script> must hash stably and 'unsafe-inline' is ignored only when hashes present, so a missed page silently breaks; ship the report-only/sink first (next item) then enforce. On-brand (verifiable self-enforcement), constraint-safe.

verifiedProposals.title: Self-attesting boot record: signed /ready-exposed provenance receipt of the running server
verifiedProposals.reason: Grounded: /health git sha is unsigned/self-asserted (router.js:1614), and ensureSigningKey + src/ed25519.js sign/verify + attestation-report-builder canonicalize/verifyReport already exist, so reusing them for a boot attestation is real and Ed25519+SHA-256-only (no blockchain, no private-key echo). Strong reflexive fit for an evidence vendor. Plan not ship-now: it is a net-new module + endpoint + verifier-compat surface, M effort.

verifiedProposals.title: Move the /health writability probe to /ready (or cache it) so liveness stops doing disk I/O
verifiedProposals.reason: Confirmed: router.js:1601 writes+unlinks .hwtest in data_dir and events_dir on every unauthenticated /health hit; /ready exists (1730) and a 30s TTL cache pattern already exists (readProductGraph, 614). verifiedProposals.reason: Accurate: signin/signup/anon limiters declare no store (router.js:694,569,653) so they default to MemoryStore and reset on every Railway redeploy; no rate-limit-redis/ioredis/upstash dep exists in package.json, so the Redis path is a new dependency + optional store wiring (M, plan). A single app.use('/v1/admin', cidrGate) using ipKey is small and safe, env-gated so it is a no-op when ADMIN_ALLOW_CIDR is unset. Real defense-in-depth.

verifiedProposals.reason: Real reflexive gap: ensure-signing-key.js + the artifact moat sign produced artifacts, but /health git sha (router.js:1614) is unsigned/self-asserted and nothing attests the running build. floor always minimal, never null, pure, never throws. reg-risk-classify.js (W834): a SECOND parallel classifier giving tier + Article basis + gates_required over INTENDED_USE_CATALOG, overlapping W766 but with divergent keys (medical_dx vs medical_diagnosis, biometric_identify vs biometric_id). ai-act-export.js: assembles the nine Annex IV fields, stamping not_yet_disclosed for absent fields; buildGovernanceReport is tenant-fenced with per-row re-check; humanInLoopConfig validates threshold [0,10] nats. control-mapper.js: maps finding-ids to 12 frameworks (ASR, OWASP LLM+Agentic incl ASI01-ASI10, MITRE ATLAS, NIST AI RMF, EU AI Act, SOC 2, ISO 42001, ISO 27001, HIPAA mapping, NIST 800-53, CSA AICM, NIST COSAiS as explicit draft); asrCrosswalk() guarantees all 8 ASR controls carry a row; mappings framed as cross-references never certifications. framework-export.js: 11 pure formatters (CSV, Excel XML, Drata, Vanta, exec, crosswalk, SARIF 2.1, OSCAL 1.1.2, CycloneDX 1.6 AI-BOM, scorecard, model card, badge); scrubBannedWord strips the banned word from model cards. compliance-export.js (W921): tenant-fenced evidence bundle for SOC2/GDPR/EU AI Act with LIVE Art.12 logging-conformance, Art.72 post-market, signed Art.12 log-stream, Art.26(6) 184-day retention floor, Art.73 incident deadlines. regulatory-clock.html: dated six-framework tracker, maps-never-certifies framing, verbatim scope sentence in CTA caveats, dev@kolm.ai sole contact, stamped as-of 2026-06-10 with visible changelog. classifyArtifactRisk is live behind /v1/reg/classify-risk (reg-routes.js:109-116). Banned word honest/honesty appears ONLY in source comments and internal reasoning strings (none reach an emitted customer string); scrubBannedWord guards the one model-card leak path.
sota: Verified against June 2026 primary-source reporting. EU AI Act Digital Omnibus: deferral of stand-alone Annex III high-risk obligations from 2 Aug 2026 to 2 Dec 2027 (Annex I embedded products to 2 Aug 2028) is correct and current; political agreement 7 May 2026, legal effect on Official Journal publication expected before 2 Aug 2026; regulatory-clock.html states this accurately. ISO/IEC 42001:2023 certifiable now; Vanta and Drata adding 42001 support in 2026 with most orgs still in build phase; kolm's Drata/Vanta exports are well-positioned but generic (not 42001-control-keyed). Gap vs SOTA: NO coverage of GPAI / systemic-risk model obligations (AI Act Art.51-55, GPAI Code of Practice in force Aug 2025), notable for a model-platform vendor; NIST COSAiS correctly handled as draft; frontier GRC tools ship time-versioned, source-cited control libraries with freshness alerts, whereas kolm catalogs are static frozen objects with module version stamps but no per-row as_of/source_url/in_force_date. Sources: gibsondunn.com EU AI Act Omnibus postponed deadlines; genai.owasp.org OWASP Top 10 for Agentic Applications 2026; iso.org ISO 42001 explained.

currentState: Three subsystems, all real and wired. (1) Transactional email (src/email.js): Resend over plain fetch, no SDK. Two parallel stacks coexist - a legacy one (sendMail/sendWelcome/sendBillingActivated/sendBillingFailed, lines 19-118) that returns {skipped} when unconfigured and has NO outbox, and the current LM-8 stack (sendEmail + pure tEmail* templates, lines 264-578) that NEVER throws and falls back to data/email-outbox.jsonl on missing key / 4xx-5xx / network error. Templates are pure {subject,html,text} with inline 600px styling, single CTA, dev@kolm.ai contact, ASCII-safe punctuation. Lifecycle coverage is good: signup (token-based one-time key reveal, no plaintext key in body - W253 sec#5), setup-link/forgot-key (rotates key, emails one-shot reveal), report-ready (Trust link after $750 fulfillment, wired at router.js:13558-13571), compile-done, usage-alert (80/100%), refund, and dunning. From-header fallback correctly hard-coded to dev@kolm.ai (email.js:246). (2) Outbound tenant webhooks (src/webhooks.js): event-store-backed append-only subscriptions folded into current state, HMAC-SHA256 Stripe-style signatures (t=ts,v1=sig), 4 attempts with exponential backoff, 10s timeout, per-attempt delivery audit, SSRF host blocklist, frozen event allow-list. (3) Dunning (src/dunning.js): 3/7/14-day retry ladder as a tenant-fenced state machine, idempotent advance via BEGIN IMMEDIATE, suspension after final lapse, resolveDunning on payment recovery; swept in-process every KOLM_REATTEST_INTERVAL_MIN (server.js:527-547), Stripe invoice.payment_failed opens schedule (router.js:13447-13474), payment_succeeded / subscription active clears it.
sota: 2026 bulk-sender rules (Gmail/Yahoo/Microsoft, enforced since May 2025) require SPF+DKIM+DMARC alignment, RFC 8058 one-click List-Unsubscribe + List-Unsubscribe-Post for non-transactional sends, complaint rate <0.3%, hard-bounce <2%. SOTA practice: separate transactional from marketing reputation by subdomain/IP pool; maintain an APPLICATION-level suppression list driven by provider bounce/complaint webhooks (Resend exposes email.bounced / email.delivery_delayed / email.complained events, signed with Svix). Outbound-webhook SOTA (Svix/Hookdeck/Stripe 2026): exponential backoff WITH jitter to avoid thundering herd, stable idempotency-key per event so receivers can dedupe (Stripe uses 3-day dedupe TTL), ack-fast then process in a bounded-concurrency worker, and a dead-letter queue with a throttled replay tool for events that exhaust retries. Sources: resend.com/docs/dashboard/webhooks, svix.com/resources/webhook-best-practices/retries, suped.com one-click-unsubscribe, mailtrap deliverability 2026, digitalapplied webhook-reliability-2026.

currentState: kolm.ai is running two products on one domain but the public IA only presents ONE of them at the top level. The global nav (index.html lines 113-145, identical on pricing/compare/contact/how-it-works/capabilities) is 100% the Model Platform/compiler surface (B): top items are Platform, Developers, How it works, What we test, Pricing, Trust, Docs; the single CTA is "Get an API key" -> /signup. The homepage hero (index.html L159-171) is entirely compiler: eyebrow "The compiler for teams on OpenAI and Anthropic", H1 "Point your API calls. Own the model." The Agent Security Evidence surface (A) - the cryptographic-trust moat that ties the whole stack together - appears nowhere above the fold and is reachable only from a footer column titled "Audit" (index.html L526-533: /audit, /checks, /report, /audit-pricing, /solutions/ai-vendors, /solutions/enterprise-buyers). pricing.html is compiler-only (6 plans Free/Indie/Pro/Team/Business/Enterprise on two meters: gateway calls + compile credits); the LOCKED ASR pricing ($750 / $299/$999 / $25,000 / +$10,000 Deep Red-Team) does NOT appear on /pricing at all - it lives on audit-pricing.html and is partially restated inside solutions/ai-vendors.html (L415-440). There are TWO different navs: audit.html (L116-125) has its own audit-scoped nav (Overview / What we test / Pricing / Docs / Verify / Trust / + 'kolm compiler' crossover link) and a CTA 'Run the free scan'; but solutions/enterprise-buyers.html and solutions/ai-vendors.html (both surface-A pages) use the COMPILER global nav with the 'Get an API key' CTA, so a security buyer who lands there sees no audit nav and a wrong primary CTA. The surface-A pages themselves are strong in isolation: ai-vendors.html has a sharp PAS hero ('Your agent works. The deal stalled in security review. Unstall it.'), a problem->path->report->two-clocks->pricing->CTA funnel, the ASR-1..ASR-6 framework crosswalk to SOC2/ISO42001/NIST/EU AI Act/OWASP/MITRE (L340-354), the verbatim scope sentence (L355), and a competitor objection line ('How this differs from Vanta, Drata and runtime guardrails', L398). compare.html is compiler-vs-point-tools only and never positions kolm against the actual security-evidence competitors (Vanta/Drata) that ai-vendors.html names. Net: a credible, well-built funnel exists for each surface in isolation, but the top-level IA hides surface A, splits the brand across two inconsistent navs, fragments pricing, and forces a visitor to self-diagnose which of two products they want with zero routing help on the homepage.
sota: Competitive bar moved THIS WEEK: Drata announced (2026-06-10, businesswire/helpnetsecurity) it is making "AI Agent Governance" a named enterprise category - inline sensors that inventory every agent, map owner/identity/permissions/scope, and block policy violations inline. Drata cites 2.1M security questions processed via its Trust Graph with AI-specific questions up 30% in 9 months - hard validation of surface A's beachhead (stalled security reviews of AI vendors) AND a warning that incumbents are crowding in. kolm's defensible wedge vs Drata/Vanta is exactly what those platforms cannot offer: a portable, Ed25519-signed report the buyer verifies OFFLINE with no kolm server in the trust path (ai-vendors.html L144 'No kolm server in the trust path') - this is the one differentiator that must be loud, and today it is buried. 2026 B2B SaaS IA guidance (genesysgrowth, joinamply, lovable) is consistent: a homepage must answer 'what problem / what product / who is it for' within 5 seconds and deliberately omit to maximize clarity; multi-product companies should maintain clear separation between offerings while keeping conversion-focused nav; activation CTAs belong in the primary nav and peer/social proof is now the dominant trust mechanism (63% of buyers start with peers). kolm's homepage fails the 5-second test for a security buyer (they see a compiler, not an evidence product) and offers no separation/routing between the two products. Sources: businesswire.com/news/home/20260610042883, helpnetsecurity.com/2026/06/10/drata-ai-agent-governance, vanta.com/resources/vanta-vs-drata-for-enterprises, genesysgrowth.com/blog/designing-b2b-saas-homepages, joinamply.com/academy/saas-homepage-template.

currentState:
The kinst/kfig figure family is a mature, well-architected "Datasheet Machine" instrument language built almost entirely in one stylesheet (public/kolm-2026.css, ~2179 lines). It is genuinely category-strong, not placeholder art.

FRAME: .kinst (css 1897-1942) is a precise-instrument shell: layered luminance depth (--ki-depth / --ki-depth-hi multi-shadow), a masked engraved dual grid floor (::before), optional registration corner marks (.kinst--marks), a mono micro-label header (.kinst__bar with a pulsing .kinst__live dot) and an engraved footer readout (.kinst__foot). .is-verified lifts depth toward the accent. forced-colors and reduced-transparency fallbacks present.

MOTION: a clean five-primitive contract (css 1872-2003) FLOW/DRAW/SETTLE/STAMP/BREATHE keyed off [data-km~="..."] + .km-run. kolm-2026.js KMotion (~271-308) adds .km-run via IntersectionObserver, idempotent (data-km-wired), fail-open (no JS = CSS resting state IS the final frame), and every primitive has a prefers-reduced-motion:reduce static fallback. GPU-only (transform/opacity/stroke-dashoffset).

COMPONENTS in the shared system: kpipe (connected pipeline, css 2005-2052; one stretched connector rail with a single animated kflowpkt, verified is-active stage, readout chips — verify.html:310-316 exemplary), kscatter (positioning plot with gridlines, best-quadrant wash, median dividers, ring+crosshair on best point, frontier curve — runtimes.html:320, compare.html, index.html), kbars (gradient bars with draw-in growth — roi.html:450, research.html, compare.html), kspec3d (exploded isometric layered plates, leader lines, k3-join lines converging to a stamped Ed25519 seal, paper plate flips ink via :has() — spec.html:260, report.html, runtimes.html, platform.html, enterprise.html), kseal (animated ring + drawing check + pip), plus legacy vloop (valid/void trust kernel), cap (signed ledger rows), anat, ramp, icpt, kglyph, kflow, kmeter. Figures correctly map to the real signed-object schema.

ACCESSIBILITY: every figure has role="group"/role="img" with long descriptive aria-labels; decorative SVGs aria-hidden; figcaptions add plain-language reads; color is never the sole carrier (mark+text). Backdrop .bg "Captured Signal" field (css 1543-1590) left untouched per constraint.
sota: 2026 dev-infra peers (Vercel, Linear, Stripe) converge on near-black surfaces with one accent + strict contrast as the primary surface, type-dense technically-credible visuals, and documentation-grade restraint. kolm already matches this register and exceeds the typical bar with its engraved-instrument idiom. USWDS / AstroUXDS / dataviza11y guidance: SVG charts should inherit theme via CSS (kolm does, fully token-driven), cap palette complexity (kolm uses one accent + neutral ramp), and CRITICALLY provide a screen-reader-accessible data TABLE alongside any chart rather than a single aria-label string — kolm's kscatter/kbars carry only a (thorough) aria-label, the one clear gap vs 2026 dataviz-a11y SOTA. SVG-in-design-system best practice also warns about duplicate <defs> ids across inline SVGs on one page (first id wins globally), which kolm risks via copy-pasted gradient defs. Sources: designsystem.digital.gov/components/data-visualizations, astrouxds.com/patterns/data-visualization, github.com/dataviza11y/resources, line25.com/articles/best-landing-page-design-examples-2026.

It renders status pills, dunning warnings, a tax form, an invoice table, and a per-subscription "Trust link" button that opens /v1/trust/<slug>. Field contract matches the route. This page alone is ~8/10.

sota: 2026 self-serve billing norm (Stripe "Self-serve subscription management and billing portals"; designrevision SaaS Stripe 2026): Stripe handles checkout/portal/tax, the app handles access control via webhooks and renders a thin status surface. account-billing.html already matches this pattern (portal deep-link + entitlement read), which is the right architecture. A known 2026 Stripe-portal limitation is that customers cannot self-serve upgrade/downgrade when subscribed to multiple products or usage-based components (Stripe resources) - relevant here because kolm mixes a plan subscription + Continuous re-attestation + one-time Full Readiness, so plan changes correctly route to /pricing rather than the portal. For an agent-security-evidence product the category-defining surface is NOT generic billing but a trust-artifact console: peers in attestation/transparency (sigstore Rekor search UIs, SLSA provenance viewers, vendor trust portals like SafeBase/Vanta Trust Center) center the authenticated view on "here are your signed artifacts, here is each one's verify state and shareable link." kolm has the data (signed report + public_slug + offline verify) but no console renders it - the single biggest miss versus SOTA. Sources: stripe.com/resources self-serve portals; designrevision.com SaaS Stripe Integration 2026.

currentState: Five public trust surfaces, uneven in maturity. (1) public/report-viewer.html is the strongest: a real in-browser Ed25519 verifier (WebCrypto, RFC 8037) that loads a report from a trust URL / paste / drop / ?src=, verifies offline against kolm's published keyring, renders a signed-document "lens" (sticky verification masthead, evidence tier A/B/C, verbatim scope-before-findings box, 8-control posture grid grey-by-default, findings, injection probes reported-not-warranted, signature block + verify command), supports a real A-vs-B diff, View-as-JSON, and a print stylesheet; verifier source is linked openly (/kolm-audit-verify.js). (2) public/badge.html ships three copy-paste snippets (inline-SVG, shields.io Markdown, base64 data-URI image) plus a live status badge (/v1/trust/:slug/badge.svg) whose state machine (green N% ready / grey stale (Month YYYY) / report revoked / unknown) matches the actual backend route in src/audit-routes.js:1836; ASCII-clean, three-bar mark preserved, good anatomy figures. (3) public/transparency-log.html is a polished but PURELY ILLUSTRATIVE marketing page: it explains the RFC 6962-style Merkle log and shows a timeline with HARDCODED seq numbers (4128/4127/4126/4125) and a note that they are illustrative; it never fetches the live log even though src/transparency-log-routes.js exposes a full public API (/v1/transparency-log/size, /entries, /entries/:seq, /proof/:seq with top-level RFC 9162 fields, /checkpoints/latest, /checkpoints). (4) public/trust.html is the COMPILER/platform readiness-ledger page (surface B): an open gates closeout (8 P0/P1 gates), reviewer path, enterprise control posture; strong caveats discipline, sources to /product-readiness-closeout.json. (5) public/trust-center.html is the SELLER-only Trust Link analytics app (noindex): views + distinct viewers per link, API-key/session auth, viewer addresses hashed at boundary. Notably, there is NO standalone buyer-facing shareable trust HTML page in this set; the public per-report trust page is server-rendered at /v1/trust/:slug (audit-routes.js). Backend also exposes unused public richness: /v1/trust/:slug/questionnaire (pre-fills a security questionnaire from the signed report) and /v1/trust/:slug/delta (signed drift), neither surfaced in these pages.
sota: SOTA for a public transparency log is Sigstore's live, queryable rekor-search-ui at search.sigstore.dev: anyone searches by hash / log index / email / UUID, opens an entry, and sees its inclusion proof verified - a working public viewer, not an illustration. kolm's transparency-log.html is below that bar because it only describes the log; the backend (RFC 9162 inclusion proofs, signed checkpoints) is already at parity but unsurfaced. SOTA for trust pages in the compliance space is the customer-facing Trust Center shipped by Vanta, Drata (after acquiring SafeBase), and Scytale: real-time posture, self-serve evidence/document access, and an AI Q&A chatbot that answers reviewer questions from live control context (per Vanta/Drata/Scytale 2026 material). kolm's differentiator over all of them is genuinely stronger - cryptographic offline verification with no server in the trust path vs their attestational dashboards - but kolm under-sells it: the verify experience is gated behind paste/URL friction and the transparency log is not yet interactive. The verifiable-artifact UX (report-viewer.html) is at or above peer SOTA. Sources: https://github.com/sigstore/rekor-search-ui , https://search.sigstore.dev/ , https://docs.sigstore.dev/logging/overview/ , https://www.vanta.com/resources/best-soc-2-compliance-software , https://scytale.ai/resources/best-vanta-alternatives-to-consider/ , https://soc2auditors.org/insights/drata-review/
