currentState: kolm ships two parallel documentation surfaces. public/docs.html (633 lines) covers the compiler/model-platform DX with a quickstart against real HTTP routes (/v1/route/chat/completions, /v1/capture/export, /v1/compile, all confirmed present in public/openapi.json) plus a CLI block. public/audit-docs.html (919 lines) covers the Agent Security / ASR DX with a working curl quickstart against /v1/audit/scan and matching request/response JSON, a connector-detection table, ASR-1..ASR-8 controls, and a verify section. public/spec.html (698 lines) is the strongest asset: it documents the signed-report field inventory, the four detached fields (signature_ed25519, timestamp_evidence, log_checkpoint, co_signatures), the canonicalization rules (recursive key-sorted whitespace-free UTF-8, deliberately not RFC 8785), two trust tiers, three evidence grades A/B/C, three offline verifiers (Browser/Python/Go), and a versioned changelog. public/integrations.html documents an ingress->kolm->egress topology with 17 channel families. SDKs: sdk/python (kolm/audit.py, 927 lines) is a complete offline verifier reproducing the JS canonicalization byte-for-byte with a two-tier VerifyResult plus an AuditClient HTTP wrapper; sdk/go is a stdlib-only two-tier verifier with a strong README and a clear failure-mode contract; sdk/node (index.mjs, 218 lines) is compiler-only with NO offline-verify export. The core trust story (Ed25519 + Merkle, offline verifiable, multiple SDK implementations claimed byte-for-byte) is coherent and largely accurate against the shipped routes.
sota: 2026 attestation tooling has consolidated on the in-toto Attestation Framework (ITE-6) with DSSE envelopes, SLSA provenance predicates, and Sigstore (keyless OIDC signing + the Rekor transparency log); slsa.dev now publishes an ai-generation/v0.1 predicate, and there is active 2025-2026 work on AI/model provenance attestations (sigstore.dev, slsa.dev, infoq.com/news/2025/08/provenance). kolm deliberately uses raw Ed25519 + a SHA-256 Merkle transparency log with its own canonical JSON; this is defensible and constraint-required (no blockchain, no new deps), and the offline-verify-without-vendor property is genuinely strong, but it has zero interop with the in-toto/DSSE ecosystem and the spec never explains the choice, so a security reviewer cannot tell if it is a considered decision or an oversight. On API-docs DX, the Stripe benchmark remains the bar: per-language tabs on every code sample, an in-page runnable try-it with auto-injected test keys, and a 3-pane reference layout (apidog.com/blog/stripe-docs). kolm's docs are static curl-only with no language tabs and no try-it, which is a noticeable gap versus 2026 expectations even though the underlying OpenAPI file exists and is accurate.

currentState:
The compile-engine is a spec-driven model/recipe compilation pipeline with an unusually mature provenance and anti-leakage core, surrounded by several surface-level stubs and one or two genuinely incomplete subsystems.

Canonical path: compileSpec (src/spec-compile.js, 1316 lines) is the single build entrypoint. It validates the spec, splits seeds BEFORE synthesis (W283 anti-leakage), records synthesis_input_hash, scores a comparator over a HOLDOUT, emits compiled_targets, runs a post-build auditor cross-check with rollback, and re-signs in place for W726 workload_profile patching. src/compile.js wraps this for jobs (createJob/runJob: Recall -> Distill -> Decompose -> Package), refuses to compile with no positive seeds (KOLM_E_NO_SEEDS), and assigns an honest artifact_class (claude strategy -> synthesized_rule, else rule). src/compile-pipeline.js runs an 11-phase async generator (plan, tokenizer_train, corpus_prepare, dataset_split, distill, quantize, bundle, sign, verdict, install, done); _bundlePhase (W409c) enforces real row hashes, _rowOverlap disjointness, and forces production_ready:false when eval_provenance is 'placeholder'.

Provenance and class discipline are the strongest assets. src/recipe-class.js implements a real taxonomy (rule < synthesized_rule < compiled_rule < distilled_model < workflow_capsule) with a class x source_type compatibility matrix, throws on teacher+weights ambiguity, and requires a non-empty weights file on disk to claim distilled_model. The eval gate (src/compile-eval-gate.js, w-eval-gate-v1) is fail-closed: resolveKScore, regression-class delta, and embedEvalSummaryReceipt binds the eval summary hash into an Ed25519 gateway receipt output_hash with a matching verify path. src/native-compile.js does opt-in C/Rust/Wasm with reproducible-build flags (SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH=0, strip, lto, remap-path-prefix), a verifier rebuild path (rebuildBinaryFromSource), and toolchainVersionHash; missing toolchain is a non-fatal skip. src/bundle-runner.js gives a portable runtime proof (verifies entry sha256, dynamic import, Node18+/Bun/Deno).

Incomplete or stubbed: src/compile-stream.js is a hardcoded fake-progress emitter (K_SCORE_BY_PASS = {1:0.71,2:0.83,3:0.91}, done event fakes k_score:0.91 and file_size_gb:1.9) with a comment that a real compile-jobs.js will eventually wrap it. src/compile-ir.js runCompiledWorkflow THROWS on BRANCH/ARTIFACT/MEMORY_READ/MEMORY_WRITE on cache miss ('not yet supported'); parameter hoisting only binds verbatim workflow input. src/tsac-compiler.js (w721-v1) is heuristic string-matching that depends on an upstream attention-trace collector explicitly described as 'a future wave'. src/compile-simulator.js (cs-v1) is a decision wrapper over a K-score predictor, not a compiler. JS recipe generators run in node:vm with a frozen lib and cooperative timeout, with an in-code admission that node:vm 'is not a hard security boundary'. So: a hardened deterministic-provenance spine, with a fake streaming surface and two real capability gaps (workflow-IR control flow, TSAC telemetry source).
sota: SLSA build-provenance (slsa.dev, in-toto attestation envelope) is the 2026 supply-chain standard; notably SLSA explicitly DROPPED full reproducible/hermetic builds from 1.0 as 'very hard at scale' (en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Reproducible_builds, slsa.dev/spec/draft/build-provenance). This validates kolm's 'best-effort determinism' posture as industry-aligned rather than a deficiency, but kolm's per-artifact provenance is currently bespoke JSON rather than an in-toto/SLSA-shaped envelope, which limits interop with downstream verifiers. Transparency-log frontier: RFC 6962 (Certificate Transparency) is now OBSOLETED by RFC 9162; Sigstore/Rekor is the de-facto verifiable-artifact transparency log, proving each entry via a Merkle inclusion proof recomputed against a signed checkpoint/STH (docs.sigstore.dev/logging/overview, rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc6962.html). kolm's memory describes an 'RFC-6962-style Merkle log'; the public-facing language and proof shape should track RFC 9162 + signed-checkpoint semantics. Contamination/leakage frontier: 2026 work goes well beyond exact-row-hash overlap to STATISTICAL contamination tests with false-positive control (Oren et al. canonical-ordering test; Shi et al. Min-k%-Prob black-box membership; arxiv 2601.04301 'Quantifying the Effect of Test Set Contamination'). kolm's _rowOverlap is exact-match only and will miss paraphrase/near-dup leakage. Model-compilation frontier: MLIR/IREE/TPU-MLIR are the cross-target IR standard with quantization preserved in-IR (mlir.llvm.org/docs/Quantization, github.com/sophgo/tpu-mlir); kolm's compile-targets is a flat catalog with no shared IR lowering, so 'compile any model to any device' is currently catalog-deep, not IR-deep. Agent-eval CI frontier: release-gate + tool-call regression + span-level eval is now table stakes (Confident AI 2026); kolm's fail-closed eval gate is on-trend, but lacks span/tool-call granularity.

verifiedProposals.title: Compile Transparency Log with offline inclusion proofs (RFC 9162-shaped)
verifiedProposals.reason: Strong moat fit, but the heavy machinery ALREADY EXISTS: src/transparency-log.js (w921) + src/merkle.js implement RFC 6962/9162 leaf/root/inclusion proofs with Ed25519-signed checkpoints, plus transparency-log-routes.js. The real (smaller) work is appending compile artifact_hash leaves on each successful build and threading the existing proof+checkpoint into provenance/binder verify - not building a new src/compile-log.js Merkle tree from scratch. Reuse the existing module; reject the proposed reimplementation. Ed25519+SHA-256 only, no blockchain - constraint-safe.

verifiedProposals.title: Statistical leakage attestation (beyond exact-hash disjointness)
verifiedProposals.reason: Grounded: compile-pipeline.js _rowHash/_rowsHash/_rowOverlap is exact-hash only, and seed_provenance already carries a hardcoded near_duplicate_count:0 placeholder plus a leakage-aware binder seed-gate - a clean landing spot. MinHash/SimHash + canonical-ordering test in pure JS is buildable and fail-closed reuse is real. Plan not ship-now: a defensible quantified leakage bound is a real research/calibration effort, and threshold choices become a signed customer claim that must not overstate.

verifiedProposals.title: Two-party reproduce-and-attest for native compile
verifiedProposals.reason: Real: native-compile.js already exposes rebuildBinaryFromSource, getSourceFileEntries, toolchain_version_hash, deterministic flags, and binder check #13 does opt-in rebuild-and-compare under KOLM_VERIFY_REBUILD=1. A customer-facing `kolm reproduce` verb emitting a signed reproduction_attestation with a per-slot divergence manifest is buildable and on-brand. Plan, mid value: native binaries are a minority artifact class vs distilled/gguf and cross-platform non-determinism limits reach; co-signature plumbing is non-trivial. Shipped copy must avoid the word it leans on in its pitch.

verifiedProposals.title: Out-of-process recipe execution sandbox with capability ledger
verifiedProposals.reason: Real and important: verifier.js runs recipe generators via node:vm (vm.Script/createContext, 150ms cooperative timeout) with an in-code 'not a hard security boundary' admission - a true RCE/exfil surface for a security vendor. worker_threads/child_process with resourceLimits + denied net/fs handles + a signed execution_provenance capability block is buildable. Effort L (touches a core execution path, sync busy-loop kill, perf, every recipe caller) so plan, not ship-now; high value because it converts a known weakness into differentiated signed evidence.

verifiedProposals.title: Stop streaming fabricated quality numbers from compile-stream.js
verifiedProposals.reason: Confirmed and customer-facing: compile-stream.js hardcodes K_SCORE_BY_PASS {1:0.71,2:0.83,3:0.91} and a done event with k_score:0.91/file_size_gb:1.9, and router.js wires streamCompile to GET /v1/compile/stream/:job for the no-code /account/create-model wizard. For an evidence product, streaming invented quality numbers to a real screen is an existential credibility risk. Surgical, safe, high value: gate behind a demo flag and label every value 'simulated', or pull from real phase output. Ship now.

verifiedProposals.title: Make unsupported workflow-IR step kinds fail-closed and explicit at compile time
verifiedProposals.reason: Verified: runCompiledWorkflow throws 'not yet supported' on BRANCH/ARTIFACT/MEMORY_READ/MEMORY_WRITE on a cache miss, while compileWorkflowFromTraces happily emits an IR containing those kinds. Detecting unsupported kinds at compile time and downgrading the capsule class / stamping an explicit capability-coverage field (never claim coverage it cannot replay) is surgical, fail-closed, and directly on-brand for accurate-taxonomy evidence. Ship now.

verifiedProposals.title: Enforce target availability in compile-targets / fail-closed on silent skips
verifiedProposals.reason: Real: compile-targets.js is explicitly a catalog that does not check toolchain/hardware (its own header says so), and native-compile.js records absent toolchains as a silent non-fatal skip. Surfacing an explicit per-target status (compiled | skipped_no_toolchain) so a requested int4/gguf/onnx target never looks satisfied when nothing was produced is small (S) and fail-closed. Note compile-targets is a pure leaf module - keep hw probing in native-compile/job result, do not import runtime probes into the catalog. Ship now.

verifiedProposals.title: Track RFC 9162 / signed-checkpoint language in transparency claims
verifiedProposals.reason: Partly stale premise: transparency-log.js already cites RFC 6962/9162 and signed Tree Head / inclusion-proof semantics, so the framing 'update 6962-style references to 9162' overstates the gap. Still a low-risk copy/consistency pass worth doing for buyer diligence (align customer-facing strings, keep Ed25519+SHA-256, no blockchain). Low priority, plan/batch with the transparency wiring work; not independently ship-now.

verifiedProposals.title: Hide or guard TSAC as a shippable target until the attention collector exists
verifiedProposals.reason: Confirmed: tsac-compiler.js requires >=8 captures carrying attention_traces[] and returns no_attention_telemetry, with a header stating the upstream collector is a future wave and callers must hand-label. Ensuring compile-targets / spec validation never advertises TSAC sparsity as available and returns a clear capability-unavailable result is small and fail-closed. Minor caveat: TSAC is not currently in COMPILE_TARGETS, so the fix is mostly guarding any catalog/UI surface that does list it; still worth shipping.

verifiedProposals.title: Add span/tool-call granularity to the eval gate
verifiedProposals.reason: Real: evaluateAndGate gates on a single composite K-score delta + regression-class count and embedEvalSummaryReceipt binds a fixed eval_summary. Per-tool-call/per-span sub-scores are credible 2026 agent-eval table stakes and the receipt binds via output_hash so additive sub-scores fit. Plan, mid: requires real per-span eval data upstream (not just gate code) and the gateway receipt is a fixed-field schema - extra detail must ride inside the canonical eval_summary, not new receipt fields, or validateReceipt throws.

verifiedProposals.title: compile-stream.js emits hardcoded fake K-scores and file sizes on an evidence product
verifiedProposals.reason: Same verified issue as the matching improvement: hardcoded K_SCORE_BY_PASS + faked done event, wired live to /v1/compile/stream/:job via router.js for the create-model wizard. Genuine existential credibility gap for a signed-evidence product. Surgical fix (demo-flag gate + 'simulated' labels, or real phase values). Real, buildable, ship now.

verifiedProposals.title: Workflow-IR replay is incomplete for control flow and state
verifiedProposals.reason: Accurate: runCompiledWorkflow throws on BRANCH/ARTIFACT/MEMORY_READ/MEMORY_WRITE on cache miss, and parameter hoisting only binds verbatim workflow input (derived params not generalized). So workflow_capsule cannot replay most real agentic traces. The immediate fail-closed labeling is ship-now (covered above); actually implementing branch/memory replay is a larger plan-level build (delegate to interpret() or extend the runner). Plan.

verifiedProposals.title: Leakage detection is exact-row-hash only; misses near-duplicate contamination
verifiedProposals.reason: Confirmed gap: _rowOverlap is exact content-hash disjointness; seed_provenance ships near_duplicate_count:0 as an unpopulated placeholder, so the attestation overstates train/holdout independence for a paraphrase/near-dup adversary. The cited arxiv id is unverifiable and should not be quoted in shipped copy, but the underlying methodology (canonical-ordering / Min-k% / near-dup) is real. Plan - this pairs with the leakage-attestation invention.

verifiedProposals.title: Provenance is bespoke JSON, not an in-toto/SLSA envelope
verifiedProposals.reason: Real: provenance blocks across spec-compile/compile-pipeline are custom JSON bound into artifact_hash, and while binder already verifies sigstore/Rekor bundles, there is no in-toto/SLSA attestation envelope for third-party/procurement tooling to ingest. Emitting an additive in-toto-shaped statement (alongside, not replacing, the bound JSON, staying Ed25519+SHA-256, no new external trust path) widens the offline-verifiable reach. Plan, mid value: interop benefit is real but it is net-new schema + verifier work.

verifiedProposals.title: node:vm recipe sandbox is not a hard security boundary
verifiedProposals.reason: Verified in verifier.js: vm.Script + createContext + runInContext with a 150ms cooperative DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_MS and an explicit 'node:vm is not a hard security boundary' comment. For a vendor executing recipe-emitted JS, this is a legitimate RCE/exfil and DoS (sync busy-loop) gap. Same fix as the sandbox invention - process isolation + resource limits. Plan (effort L), high value.

verifiedProposals.title: TSAC compiler depends on an attention-trace collector that does not exist
verifiedProposals.reason: Confirmed by tsac-compiler.js header + no_attention_telemetry path: the sparsity-profile capability is non-functional end-to-end (collector is a future wave, callers must hand-label). Correct fix is the small fail-closed guard above: never surface TSAC as a shippable/available target. Ship the guard now; the collector itself is the larger deferred build.

verifiedProposals.title: compile-targets catalog does not enforce hardware/toolchain availability
verifiedProposals.reason: Accurate: compile-targets.js validates slug + required manifest fields only (its header explicitly defers hw checks to runtimeAvailable), and native-compile.js silently skips absent toolchains - so int4/gguf/onnx can return a successful-looking job that produced nothing for that target. Surface explicit per-target compiled/skipped status in the job result (keep the catalog a pure leaf). Same fix as the availability improvement; ship now.

currentState:
The distillation surface is broad and genuinely multi-objective at the SHELL level, with strong provenance/governance plumbing - well above a typical demo, but with a real gap between the labels the orchestrator records and the loss the default training path actually computes.

Orchestrator (src/distill-pipeline.js, 1156 lines): prepareDistillCorpus() builds prompt->response pairs from event-store with tenant scoping (W411/W422), approved-only mode with fixed_output override, since-delta retraining, and a fail-closed holdout_only chokepoint enforced at two boundaries (corpus split + the distill() seeds write). distill() is an async-iterator that spawns workers/distill/distill.mjs detached, with W459 teacher-fallback (ordered attempt list, per-attempt manifest classification), W708-2 open-weights/proprietary teacher classification (safe-deny on unknown, KOLM_TEACHER_SOURCE=open-weights filter that throws if no eligible teacher), W718 Teacher-Council re-ranking, W787 efficiency env wiring, W808 post-distill regression gate (K-Score drop>0.02 / CFR increase>0.01 -> rollback verdict), W832 meta-trainer row emission, and run-meta/progress.jsonl/manifest persistence with listDistillRuns/readDistillRun read surfaces. CRITICAL FINDING: the three MODES (kd_softmax/kd_top_k/rejection_sampling) are passed to the worker only as --distillation-method (a manifest label); the worker's full mode spawns scripts/train_lora.py which is a plain SFT/LoRA trainer with NO softmax/top-k/logit KD loss. The real generalized-JSD on-policy loss lives in a separate scripts/train_gkd.py (wraps trl.GKDTrainer, has generalized_jsd_loss with beta interpolation) that distill() never spawns. So the headline distillation objectives are recorded, not differentiated, in the default path.

Strategy planner (distill-strategy.js): a 10-strategy scored catalog (collect/rule-first/classifier/lora_sft/kd_top_k/kd_softmax/rejection/preference/onpolicy/spec-decode) with feasibility blockers (min pairs, holdout, privacy mode, teacher) and a heuristic scorer. Solid product-grade router.

Objective shells: distill-grpo.js (GRPO/RLVR with verifiable reward families code_exec/math/schema/format/kolm_verifier, loss types grpo/bnpo/dr_grpo, in-repo train_grpo.py), distill-onpolicy.js (white-box reverse-KL shell + W921 ROPD black-box rubric-based path with in-repo apps/trainer/ropd.py), distill-preference.js (DPO/SimPO/ORPO/KTO + pure-JS preference pair mining from council disagreement + KTO row conversion), progressive-distill.js (3-pass F/R/E capability gating with need-more gap estimator), vlm-distill.js (queue-only, honest no-key envelope). All return honest no_trainer_installed/deferred envelopes when torch/trl absent. Verified the Python backing is real and extensive (apps/trainer/ has grpo.py, ropd.py, preference.py, distill.py, gkd-style trainers, lora_variants with PiSSA/DoRA, online_dpo.py, contrastive/distillm2 loss tests).

Provenance (distill-provenance.js): recomputes training-pairs hash from disk, refuses on hash drift, emits buildLineage source=distillation (teacher+student) or falls back to source=rebuild when stub - so no false distillation claim. Carries teacher_holdout_accuracy, redaction_map_hash, teacher_call_log_hash, reinjection_log_hash for verifier replay - this is the tie-back to surface A's trust moat.

Governance: distill-approval-queue.js (W782 manager sign-off, pending->granted/rejected/expired, webhook+email notify, tenant-fenced, store-backed), secure-training.js (classifies corpus via privacy-membrane, refuses sensitive data -> third-party pod without BYOC/airgap/override, teardown shred policy), cloud-distill.js (managed-distill job lifecycle + separate GPU-hour training meter, honest no_pool_configured default).

Efficiency (distill-efficiency.js): real W787 early-stop/precision/grad-checkpoint with env wiring VERIFIED in train_lora.py (reads KOLM_PRECISION/KOLM_GRAD_CHECKPOINT/KOLM_EARLY_STOP_* and wires EarlyStoppingCallback), plus W921 LoRA-variant/GaLore/packing normalizer with PiSSA snapshot/convert VERIFIED in train_lora.py. data-curate.js: 6-stage gated curation (quality/minhash/dedup/cluster/cot/pii + opt-in learned quality classifier, semantic cluster, confident-learning label-error detection, diversity select) with honest degradation and report blocks.
sota: On-policy distillation (OPD) is now standard in 2025-2026 post-training: Qwen3, MiMo, GLM-5 all adopt OPD; MiniLLM (reverse-KL, mode-seeking) and GKD (arXiv:2306.13649, generalized JSD interpolating on/off-policy) are the canonical objectives - kolm has the GKD loss in train_gkd.py but does not wire it into the main orchestrator. 2026 frontier: "Rethinking On-Policy Distillation" (arXiv:2604.13016) gives a phenomenology+recipe; "Distillation Traps and Guards" (arXiv:2604.18963) introduces a distillability calibration knob; HDPO (arXiv:2603.23871) hybrid distillation policy optimization with privileged self-distillation. Speculative-decoding distillation matured: SpecKD (arXiv:2510.24021) interleaved teacher/student sampling, OmniDraft cross-vocab n-gram cache for one-drafter-any-target, Draft-OPD replays drafting from verification-exposed error positions training on accepted+rejected proposals, LK-losses target acceptance-rate directly rather than KL proxy. kolm lists speculative_decoding_train as a strategy but I saw no acceptance-rate-targeted draft trainer. LoRA-variant SOTA all present in kolm: PiSSA (2404.02948), DoRA (2402.09353), rsLoRA, LoRA+, GaLore (2403.03507), NEFTune, sample-packing. Competitive landscape: OpenPipe/Together/Predibase offer managed distill+SFT; kolm's differentiator is signed provenance + the K-score-as-reward train-eval-unification (GRPO reward == eval verifier), which is genuinely novel for an agent-security-evidence product. Sources: github.com/chrisliu298/awesome-on-policy-distillation, arxiv.org/html/2604.13016v1, arxiv.org/html/2510.24021v1, arxiv.org/pdf/2306.13649, arxiv.org/html/2604.18963.

verifiedProposals.title: Signed Distillation Fidelity Receipt: cryptographically bind the OBJECTIVE that actually ran to the loss the trainer executed
verifiedProposals.reason: Real: confirmed distillation_method is recorded but train_lora.py runs plain SFT regardless (worker line 319-329), and provenance binds the method into lineage. Directly extends the Ed25519+Merkle moat. Buildable but spans worker+Python trainers+provenance+verifier (effort M is honest); depends on the train_gkd.py routing fix landing first, so plan not ship-now. Rename any 'honest' wording in code/comments to Caveats per constraint.

verifiedProposals.title: K-Score-as-reward closed loop with provenance-bound train==eval guarantee across ALL objectives
verifiedProposals.reason: Grounded: REWARD_FAMILIES includes 'kolm_verifier' (distill-grpo.js line 28) and W808 gate exists. But the claim is aspirational on this stack: the GRPO reward lives in Python (apps/trainer/reward.py) while the W808 gate is Node reading progress.jsonl, so a single byte-identical 'scorer_sha256' shared by trainer and gate is a cross-language refactor, not a wiring. Worth doing but larger than stated; plan.

verifiedProposals.title: Honest-curve regression gate: never emit a promote/rollback verdict off a synthesized K-Score
verifiedProposals.reason: Real and high-value: W808 gate reads k_score from progress.jsonl rows that distill() synthesizes via kAccum interpolation (lines 726-732), so a fabricated curve can drive promote/rollback today. Surgical: tag synthetic rows measured:false, short-circuit _w808RegressionGate to a non-binding verdict. Constraint note: title says 'Honest-curve'; ship the field as eval_status=unmeasured (already proposed) and avoid the forbidden word in any surfaced string.

verifiedProposals.title: Spawn train_gkd.py for softmax/top-k modes so the objective is real
verifiedProposals.reason: Confirmed core P0: train_gkd.py with generalized_jsd_loss/GKDTrainer exists but distill()->distill.mjs full mode only spawns train_lora.py (line 319-329). Either route to train_gkd.py or downgrade the recorded method to 'sft'. The downgrade half is ship-now-small; the real GKD routing is M (needs trl GKDTrainer plumbing + full-mode branch), so plan. The truthful-downgrade fallback should land immediately regardless.

verifiedProposals.title: Wire W921 trainer-variant env into distill()
verifiedProposals.reason: Accurate: distill() builds/spreads _efficiencyEnv (W787, lines 507/714) but never calls normalizeTrainerVariantOptions/buildTrainerVariantEnv (W921), so PiSSA/DoRA/GaLore/packing are unreachable from the primary entrypoint though both helpers are exported from distill-efficiency.js. Add 4 opts + one env spread mirroring the W787 pattern. Small, safe, additive.

verifiedProposals.title: Extend distill-provenance to accept the RL/preference run-meta shape
verifiedProposals.reason: Real moat gap: loadDistillProvenance hard-throws unless worker=='kolm-distill-worker' (line 71); GRPO/ROPD/DPO write separate run-meta.json shapes with no signed lineage. High value to extend trust to RL/preference objectives. M because buildLineage demands teacher+student_base and the RL run-meta shape differs; needs a careful second adapter, not a one-liner.

verifiedProposals.title: Enforce approval as a gate inside distill()
verifiedProposals.reason: Confirmed: getApprovalStatus is exported (distill-approval-queue.js line 430) but distill() never calls it; the only approval refs in the pipeline are the unrelated approvedOnly corpus filter. Adding an env/tenant-gated check at the top of distill() that returns a needs_approval envelope is small and safe (default-off preserves current callers).

verifiedProposals.title: Implement an actual rejection-sampling loop or remove the mode
verifiedProposals.reason: Verified: worker collect loop calls the teacher once per row (distill.mjs lines 252-299); no N-candidate generation or judge filter, while scoreCandidateLocal exists only in distill-preference.js. Mode is advertised in MODES + strategy catalog. The honest fix (implement N-candidate + judge) is M; the cheap fix (drop the unimplemented mode) is small but a product/strategy decision, so plan.

verifiedProposals.title: Add measured-vs-synthetic flag to progress rows now
verifiedProposals.reason: Smallest correct fix and the foundation for the gate fix: tag the synthesized evt at lines 727-733 with measured:false. One-field change, zero risk, immediately lets the gate/dashboard/receipt distinguish a real eval curve from interpolation. Rename the 'honesty' framing to Caveats in any comment.

verifiedProposals.title: Orchestrator distillation MODES are labels, not loss paths - kd_softmax/kd_top_k/rejection_sampling all run plain SFT
verifiedProposals.reason: Fully verified P0 for a provenance product: distill() passes --distillation-method=${pipeline_mode} (line 703) but full mode spawns train_lora.py (SFT) not train_gkd.py; the manifest then records a method the trainer did not execute, and that method is bound into the signed lineage. This is the highest-severity gap in the set. Fix is M (route to train_gkd.py or downgrade method).

verifiedProposals.title: Rejection_sampling mode has no rejection-sampling implementation
verifiedProposals.reason: Confirmed: collect/full worker path generates one teacher response per pair; no N-candidate+judge loop; scoreCandidateLocal is unwired to the main pipeline. Accurate gap; fix is the same plan-level work as the rejection-sampling improvement above.

verifiedProposals.title: distill() does not wire W921 LoRA-variant/GaLore/packing env, only W787 efficiency env
verifiedProposals.reason: Verified: only buildEfficiencyEnv(W787) is spread into the spawn env; buildTrainerVariantEnv(W921) is exported but never called from distill(). Same finding as the W921 improvement; small surgical fix.

verifiedProposals.title: No coupling between objective shells (GRPO/ROPD/preference/onpolicy) and the main distill() iterator or the W808 regression gate / provenance chain
verifiedProposals.reason: Accurate: trainGrpo/trainRopd/trainPreference write to separate ~/.kolm/*-runs dirs with their own run-meta.json, bypassing loadDistillProvenance (worker-tag throw, line 71) and _w808RegressionGate. The trust moat genuinely does not extend to RL/preference objectives. Real and important; M-to-L to unify.

verifiedProposals.title: W808 regression gate K-Score is synthetic in collect/stub modes
verifiedProposals.reason: Verified: _w808ExtractKscoreFromRunDir falls back to progress.jsonl, whose rows are synthesized by the kAccum interpolation (lines 726-732) when no real eval ran, so the gate can emit promote/rollback off a fabricated curve. Highest-confidence trust risk; fixed by the measured-flag + gate short-circuit pair, both small.

verifiedProposals.title: No acceptance-rate-targeted speculative-draft trainer despite spec-decode strategy
verifiedProposals.reason: Partly real: strategy catalog advertises speculative_decoding_train and apps/trainer/speculative.py exists, but I found no acceptance-rate objective wiring connecting them (no 'acceptance' refs in speculative.py or the router). Lower priority than the objective-fidelity cluster; a draft-trainer with an acceptance-rate target is genuine new work (plan/L).

verifiedProposals.title: Approval queue is not enforced as a gate before distill() runs
verifiedProposals.reason: Confirmed: getApprovalStatus exists/exported but distill() never checks a granted row before spawning. Same finding as the approval-enforcement improvement; small, env/tenant-gated, default-off so existing local callers are unaffected.

currentState: The training surface is broad and largely real, split between a Node orchestration layer (src/) and a genuinely deep Python trainer suite (apps/trainer/, ~40 modules). Node layer read in full: (1) src/pipeline-train.js - an async-iterator `train()` that runs the local distill worker (workers/distill/distill.mjs) or polls cloud jobs (/v1/jobs/:id), parses [progress] epoch=N k=0.XX markers, and implements holdout-K auto-stop on two consecutive epoch drops with SIGINT to the child. Wired into cli/kolm.js (`kolm pipeline-train --watch`). (2) src/training-planner.js - `plan(datasetId)` does real heuristic dataset analysis: task detection (classification/extraction/redaction/generation), label entropy/diversity, input-length p50/p95, sensitive-data regex, then picks a path (rule_first/classifier/lora/distill) + backbone + cost/latency/replacement estimates with warnings (synthetic-in-holdout, few_real_examples, sensitive_data_in_distill). Wired into router.js + cli + consumed by compile-pipeline/distill-pipeline. (3) src/kolm-meta-trainer.js - a pure-JS meta-model predicting kscore/compile_time/failure_mode from 10 frozen features. Default engine is a toy depth-1 stump GBM; a real depth-D GBM engine (src/gbm-regressor.js) plus split-conformal calibration (Angelopoulos-Bates) was built (W921) and is opt-in via engine:'gbm'/KOLM_META_ENGINE, with feature-order schema fence and tenant-fenced row reads. (4) src/tokenizer-train.js - Node API over workers/tokenizer-train/train.mjs (pure-JS BPE/unigram/wordpiece), with loadTokenizer encode/decode + tokenizerStats (compression_vs_gpt4, oov_rate). (5) src/active-learning.js + active-learning-queue.js - four-signal capture richness scorer (importance/weakness/novelty/recency), coverage-gap detector with production-demand weighting, recommend-next-captures, feed-to-self-improvement, and a queue that enqueues teacher/mixed routing decisions for the next distill pass; tenant-fenced with insufficient-data envelopes (MIN_CAPTURES_FOR_GAPS=30). (6) src/constrained-decode.js - JSON-Schema/grammar/regex guided decode via workers/constrained (outlines/lm-format-enforcer), honest no_constrained_decoder envelope when deps absent. (7) src/marketplace-finetune.js - transfer-learning entrypoint that copies a marketplace artifact + queues a distill run (status:'queued' only; does not spawn the worker). (8) src/tool-training-format.js - pure ChatML+tool-call training formatter + shape-only tool-schema validation. (9) src/accelerate.js - student-as-draft speculative decoding orchestrator + acceptance-rate bench, refuses loudly (no_kernel) when no backend wired. The Python suite is the real strength: distill.py (token-level KD + on-policy refs), grpo.py (verifiable-reward GRPO sharing the K-score reward code path), preference.py (DPO/ORPO/KTO/SIMPO/IPO selector), plus contrastive/online_dpo/progressive/moe/vlm/long_context/federated trainers, all well-cited. All 'honest'/'honesty' occurrences are in internal code comments only - none leak to customer/API response strings or public/*.html, so no hard-constraint violation found.
sota: 2026 frontier for this domain has moved decisively to on-policy / student-conditioned distillation: Thinking Machines Lab "On-Policy Distillation" (2025), Self-Distilled Reasoner / OPSD (arXiv:2601.18734, 2026), and Relaxed On-Policy Distillation (arXiv:2603.11137) all train on trajectories sampled from the STUDENT policy to kill exposure bias, with dense per-token teacher feedback rather than outcome-only reward. A recurring 2026 finding: GRPO stagnates from reward-diversity collapse (>50% of batches hit zero reward std within 100 steps), motivating dense student-conditioned supervision (RLRT upweights student-divergent-but-correct tokens, +8.9% avg on math). kolm's apps/trainer/distill.py cites the on-policy literature and grpo.py exists, but the Node planner/pipeline default path is still teacher-token KD; on-policy is not the planner's recommended default. On the trust side, the 2026 ML supply-chain standard is in-toto/SLSA-style signed provenance binding model weights to training context+datasets, with cryptographic attestation at training completion and a verification gate before deploy (slsa.dev attestation-model; arXiv:2602.19021 LLM model supply-chain; arXiv:2603.02512 human-certified module repos). kolm already owns the exact primitives this calls for (Ed25519 fa56 key + RFC-6962-style Merkle transparency log + offline verify) on surface A but does NOT yet apply them to surface-B training artifacts.

verifiedProposals.title: Signed Training Receipt: bind every distilled artifact to the fa56 Ed25519 key + Merkle log
verifiedProposals.reason: All cited primitives are real (ed25519.js buildSignatureBlock/loadOrCreateDefaultSigner, merkle.js, transparency-log.js TransparencyLog.append, gbm path), and this is the brief's explicit A<->B moat tie; but it is genuinely M not S because compile-pipeline today only writes an OPTIONAL raw ed.sign sidecar via KOLM_SIGNING_KEY (not the canonical signer) and never appends to the transparency log, so a real receipt+manifest+verifier is net-new work. ASCII fields, Ed25519+SHA256 only, no blockchain - constraint-safe.

Plan, do not ship blind.

verifiedProposals.title: Calibrated training forecast at plan time using the conformal GBM engine
verifiedProposals.reason: Real: inferKolmMeta + split-conformal CI ([pred-Q,pred+Q], coverage_target, undercalibrated flag) exist in kolm-meta-trainer.js and gbm-regressor.js fit/predict match the 10-feature META_FEATURES vector. Worthwhile credibility lift for the $750 funnel but multi-file - plan.

verifiedProposals.title: Activate the real GBM engine on the retrain route
verifiedProposals.reason: Verified exactly: src/meta-routes.js line 66 calls trainKolmMeta({ rows }) with no engine, and trainKolmMeta already accepts engine:'gbm'/KOLM_META_ENGINE with a built-in MIN_ROWS_FOR_GBM_ENGINE fallback to the stump, so passing engine and returning env.conformal is a safe surgical wire-up that unlocks already-tested capability. Backward compatible via the row-floor downgrade. ASCII-safe. Highest EV item in the set.

verifiedProposals.title: Add patience + min-delta to the overfit auto-stop
verifiedProposals.reason: Verified: recordEpoch (line 134-141) and trainCloud (line 218-224) both use the brittle last3[2].k<last3[1].k<last3[0].k rule with bestK already tracked, so swapping to a patience/min-delta window keyed off bestK is a localized, low-risk change in both loops. Standard early-stopping semantics, no constraint surface touched.

verifiedProposals.title: Close the marketplace-finetune loop with a queue consumer
verifiedProposals.reason: Real gap: marketplace-finetune.js appends provider 'kolm_marketplace_finetune_queued' and returns status:'queued', and distill-pipeline.js imports listEvents from event-store so a poller is feasible. But 'flip the row to completed/failed' is non-trivial - event-store appends are typically append-only, so status transitions need a new completion event or status model, plus actually spawning the distill worker. M effort, not a one-liner; plan.

verifiedProposals.title: Document the tokenizer encode approximation in the public-facing stat
verifiedProposals.reason: Verified: loadTokenizer().encode (line 170-203) is a greedy longest-match scan over sortedByLen, not rank-ordered BPE merges, and the line 148 comment overclaims 'matches the BPE merge semantics'; compression_vs_gpt4 is computed on that encoder. Labeling the stat 'approximate (greedy)' is a tiny ASCII-safe truthfulness fix that prevents an over-claim feeding the $750 report. The full rank-order rewrite is the M part - ship the label now, defer the rewrite.

verifiedProposals.title: Surface sensitive-data warning as a hard gate, not just a string, on the distill path
verifiedProposals.reason: Verified: planner already pushes 'sensitive_data_in_distill' (line 187) referencing KOLM_LLM_PROVIDER, so adding require_local_teacher:true to the plan return is trivial; the load-bearing half is the distill-pipeline guard that actually refuses third-party teacher calls, which must be checked against how the teacher provider is selected to avoid breaking existing runs. Strengthens the privacy posture surface A audits - on-brand. Plan so the guard gets tested.

verifiedProposals.title: Trained/distilled model artifacts are NOT cryptographically signed or Merkle-logged - the A<->B moat tie is missing
verifiedProposals.reason: PARTIALLY OVERSTATED: compile-pipeline.js DOES have a sign phase (_signPhase, line 418) that writes an OPTIONAL .ed25519.sig sidecar when KOLM_SIGNING_KEY is set, so 'zero references to the Ed25519 signing path' is false for compile-pipeline (true only for distill-pipeline). The REAL, accurate gap is narrower: no Merkle transparency-log append, no canonical training-context manifest, and the sidecar uses raw ed.sign(KOLM_SIGNING_KEY) not surface A's loadOrCreateDefaultSigner/buildSignatureBlock - so artifacts are not bound to the same fa56 transparency root. verifiedProposals.title: The real GBM meta-engine + conformal intervals are built but never activated through the HTTP/CLI retrain path
verifiedProposals.reason: Verified precisely: meta-routes.js line 66 trains with no engine arg so POST /v1/meta/retrain always builds the stump and never emits the conformal block, while trainKolmMeta + gbm-regressor + split-conformal calibration are fully built and tested. verifiedProposals.title: On-policy distillation is not the planner's default recommendation despite being 2026 SOTA and present in apps/trainer

verifiedProposals.title: marketplace-finetune only queues - no worker ever consumes the queued row in this surface
verifiedProposals.reason: Verified: marketplace-finetune.js appends 'kolm_marketplace_finetune_queued' status 'ok', returns status:'queued' with the exact forecast_note quoted, and distill-pipeline.js has no reader for that provider/feedback shape (it only listEvents for corpus prep). verifiedProposals.title: Tokenizer encode is a greedy longest-match scan, O(vocab) per char, diverging from trained BPE merge order
verifiedProposals.reason: Verified line-for-line: encode iterates sortedByLen (vocab by length desc) per position - greedy longest-match, not rank-ordered BPE merges - and the line 148 comment claims it 'matches the BPE merge semantics', which is the over-claim. compression_vs_gpt4 is computed against this approximate encoder. verifiedProposals.title: Auto-stop overfit rule is brittle (exactly two strictly-monotone drops) and resets nothing on noise
verifiedProposals.reason: Verified: pipeline-train.js requires last3[2].k<last3[1].k && last3[1].k<last3[0].k with no patience/min-delta/smoothing and holdout K as the only stop signal, in both recordEpoch and the trainCloud loop. Accurate; the patience-window fix (#5) is surgical and safe to ship.

currentState:
A deep, security-conscious, "Caveats-first" device/runtime layer in src/, but one whose strongest pieces are not yet wired into the live run path, and whose runtime artifacts are not yet bound to surface A's signing+transparency moat.

Detection and registry: src/devices.js holds DEVICES (training rigs 5090/4090/3090/A100/H100/H200, Apple M3 Max/M2 Pro, edge/mobile iPhone/Pixel/Galaxy, Jetson Orin/Pi5, TEE devices) plus a separate W409s PROFILES axis. detectLocal() only probes nvidia-smi + macOS sysctl; matchGpuName() is NVIDIA-only - no AMD/Intel/Windows-NVIDIA path there. Mobile PROFILES are runtime_status:'foundation' ("No iOS/Android runtime ships yet"). src/forge-hardware.js DOES cover nvidia/amd/apple/win-dxgi/cpu plus dtypesForComputeCapability/willArtifactFit/modelFitGrid/kvCacheSize, so the broad detection exists but is duplicated and not shared with devices.detectLocal. src/device-caps.js (deviceCaps over ssh/local with free/df/nvidia-smi/cpuinfo parsers) and src/device-capabilities.js (W372 fleet API + W888-C detectHardwareRemote/healthCheck/pingAll/recordDeployment, meetsRequirement gating VRAM/runtime/modality/TEE) form a third detection surface.

Install/registry/ssh/daemon: src/device-install.js (local/scp/HTTP-PUT, streaming sha256, ssh flag-injection guards), src/device-registry.js (atomic data/devices.json, soft/hard delete, heartbeat), src/device-ssh.js (ssh2 with hostVerifier fingerprint pinning, key_path only), and src/device-daemon.js (OFFLINE ed25519+sha256 verifyLocal, atomic applyUpdate that refuses unverified, never auto-installs) are the most mature, well-guarded part of the component.

Runtime brain: src/runtime-placement.js (W826, pure placementDecision over VRAM->RAM->NVMe->network: full_gpu/hybrid/nvme_mmap/cpu_only) carries TODO(future-wave-runtime) - it is NOT wired into the executor src/runtime.js getCompiled(); free_gb is assumed 95% at boot and never refreshed from live nvidia-smi memory.free. src/runtime-perf-estimate.js (W826-4 curve-fit K_QUANT/sqrt(params_b) x PLACEMENT_PENALTY) reserves source:'cached_run' but never implements it - the perf_sample feedback loop is an explicit TODO. src/runtime-policy.js (W372 ladder), src/runtime-confidence-router.js (W709 token entropy, nats, threshold log(2), emits no_entropy_signal_available rather than silently always-teacher), and src/runtime-sanitizer.js (W762 block/redact/fallback_to_teacher/passthrough, never silent passthrough on adversarial) are solid and self-contained, but the confidence router and sanitizer are DI seams the operator must wire.

Quant + placement of weights: src/quantization-oracle.js (constraint ranking with execution_status flags) and src/quantize-bakeoff.js (Pareto bakeoff shelling to workers/quantize) where kscore is a SURROGATE (avgBits/8 + coverage) unless KOLM_BAKEOFF_SCORE_CMD is set.

Cloud/BYOC/TEE: src/byoc.js (HMAC-signed manifests + attestation callback, TARGETS fly/aws-nitro/gcp-cvm/azure-cvm/docker) and src/cloud-compute-broker.js (lane scoring, secret_values_included:false enforced). public/runtimes.html markets "6 runtime families / 18 named runtimes" (vLLM/SGLang/TensorRT-LLM/Triton/TGI, hosted GPU, llama.cpp/Ollama/LM Studio/MLX/MLC, ONNX/OpenVINO/TVM, Core ML/LiteRT/ExecuTorch, KServe/Ray/Bento) with a cost/1k vs p50-latency ramp and exploded .kolm view; copy is clean and ASCII-safe.

Net: the runtime passport (src/runtime-passport.js) emits tested/estimated/unsupported rows that are NOT signed and NOT Merkle-logged, so surface B's central "where it runs and how fast" claim has no cryptographic tie to surface A's Ed25519 + SHA-256 transparency log - the single biggest leverage gap.
sota: On-device runtime landscape 2026: ExecuTorch is now production at Meta scale (50KB base, 12+ backends Apple/Qualcomm/Arm/MediaTek/Vulkan, ~80% of popular edge LLMs work OOTB); llama.cpp/GGUF is the de-facto CPU/local standard; MLX leads Apple Silicon; LiteRT-LM is the Android default with NPU/NNAPI delegates (v-chandra.github.io/on-device-llms, edgeaistack.ai/blog/edge-llm-runtime-stack-2026). Quantization SOTA has moved to 4-bit float: NVFP4/MXFP4/MXFP8 - MLX now ships nvfp4; reported M5 Max Qwen3.5-35B-A3B prefill 1154->1810 tok/s and decode 58->112 tok/s under NVFP4 (rockyshikoku.medium.com). KV-cache compression is mainstream: KIVI (group+residual quant), SnapKV/H2O/Ada-KV (attention-based eviction), EVICPRESS, Expected/INT8 KV (arxiv 2512.14946, 2510.00636, 2601.04719); PagedAttention + SGLang RadixAttention + continuous batching are production-standard. Model provenance is now a standard: Sigstore model-transparency signs models with DSSE/in-toto statements + SLSA provenance for build/train integrity (github.com/sigstore/model-transparency; next.redhat.com 2025/04). Confidential-compute attestation has converged on IETF RATS/EAT: AMD SEV-SNP v4 reports, Intel TDX, and crucially NVIDIA GPU TEE attestation (NRAS/nvtrust) now compose with CPU TEE attestation in one workflow via Intel Trust Authority; EAT device-attestation profiles (draft-poirier-rats-eat-da, Arm CCA token) standardize evidence (docs.nvidia.com/nvtrust, datatracker.ietf.org/wg/rats). Implication for kolm: its byoc.js attestation callback and devices TEE flags predate EAT/NRAS-shaped evidence; its runtime passport predates the model-signing-as-table-stakes shift; its perf estimates predate the NVFP4/KV-compression numbers it benchmarks against.

verifiedProposals.title: Signed Device Readiness Passport on the transparency log
verifiedProposals.reason: Real and on-brand: transparency-log.js (append/signTreeHead/verifyInclusionProof), merkle.js (RFC6962) and ed25519.js all exist, so signing a canonicalized passport + Merkle leaf reuses the genuine moat. Buildable as a canonicalize/sign/log path. Caveat: avoid the name 'Readiness Report/Receipt' colliding with the $750 ASR product - call it a runtime passport, keep sig__ok semantics, ASCII only, no 'honesty' on any surface. Plan not ship-now: the value of a 'tested' receipt depends on the perf/placement loop which is currently unwired in this Node monolith (runtime.js is a JS/WASM concept executor, not a model loader).

verifiedProposals.title: Live placement enforcement with a refreshed memory probe at load
verifiedProposals.reason: Hallucinated wire-up: the proposal (echoing a stale TODO) says to gate the load in src/runtime.js getCompiled(), but that function compiles a JS/WASM function from version.source and never loads model weights - there is no model-load run path in this monolith to enforce against (models run via the Python quantize worker / external runtimes / BYOC). The forceRefresh memory probe is itself buildable, but 'live placement enforcement at load' as described has no consumer here. The pure refresh+placement piece is captured by the smaller improvement #7.

verifiedProposals.reason: Same hallucinated premise: 'after a real inference ... emit perf_sample from src/runtime.js after run completion' - but runtime.js runs compiled concept functions, not model inference, so there is no in-process inference completion to hook. cached_run is genuinely reserved in runtime-perf-estimate.js, but wiring a measured-vs-forecast loop requires a real serving path that does not exist in this stack. Without that, this is L-effort speculative work. Reject until a real serve loop exists; the constant-refresh sub-point survives as #9.

verifiedProposals.title: EAT/NRAS attestation adapter for BYOC and TEE devices
verifiedProposals.reason: Real base: packages/attestation has nitro/sev-snp/tdx/gcp/azure parsers (currently TOFU, signing-chain stubbed) and devices.js already tags nras/pccs/snp-report; byoc.js recordAttestation already calls parseAttestation. Adding standards-shaped EAT/NRAS/SEV-SNP-v4/TDX claim parsing is buildable and aligns kolm with the framework (not a private one). Caveat: keep no-blockchain constraint (Ed25519+SHA256 only), keep the scope sentence verbatim where shown, and DROP the 'fold into signed passport' half until the passport-signing path lands. verifiedProposals.title: Unified hardware truth + auditable detection receipt
verifiedProposals.reason: Real drift: devices.detectLocal (NVIDIA+macOS only, matchGpuName NVIDIA-only), forge-hardware.detectHardware (nvidia/amd/apple/win-dxgi/cpu), device-caps.deviceCaps and device-capabilities all probe independently - four overlapping surfaces. Consolidating onto a forge-hardware-backed probe is buildable and removes a real bug class. The 'signed detection receipt' add-on is plausible (ed25519 exists) but optional. Plan (M): touches many callsites; needs tests asserting all legacy entry points return the unified shape. The signing flourish should not gate the consolidation.

verifiedProposals.title: Replace surrogate kscore with measured score by default, label proxy clearly
verifiedProposals.reason: Verified in quantize-bakeoff.js: scoreJaccard returns avgBits/8*0.9 + coverage*0.1 surrogate unless KOLM_BAKEOFF_SCORE_CMD is set, yet the Pareto 'best quant' is driven by it. Marking results 'estimated quality (proxy)' and surfacing a Constraints line is surgical and protects against silently shipping a size-driven pick as if it were measured quality. Use 'Caveats/Constraints', never the forbidden word, ASCII only. High value, small, safe - ship.

verifiedProposals.title: Refresh free memory before placement instead of trusting the 95% boot assumption
verifiedProposals.reason: Real: detectMemoryHierarchy hardcodes free_gb = vram_gb*0.95 at boot with an explicit comment that the runtime should refresh it, and there is no refresh path. Adding forceRefresh (re-shell nvidia-smi --query-gpu=memory.free) is buildable and harmless. Downgraded to plan, not ship-now, because the only caller that would benefit (placement at model load) is not wired in this stack - so the fix is correct but currently low-impact. Build it with the consolidation work.

verifiedProposals.title: Unify NVIDIA/AMD/Intel/Windows detection by delegating devices.detectLocal to forge-hardware
verifiedProposals.reason: Verified: devices.detectLocal probes only nvidia-smi + macOS sysctl and matchGpuName is NVIDIA-only, while forge-hardware.detectHardware already covers nvidia/amd/apple/win-dxgi/cpu. Delegating the GPU/dtype layer removes a genuine degraded-match bug for Windows/AMD/Intel users. M effort because detectLocal returns a registry-id shape (matched against DEVICES) while forge-hardware returns a vendor/vram/dtype shape - the mapping must be reconciled carefully. Plan; sequence with the unification invention #5.

verifiedProposals.title: Update perf curve constants and document the calibration source
verifiedProposals.reason: Real: K_QUANT anchors to a 2025 RTX-5090 NF4 run (int4=65) and has no nvfp4/mxfp4 key; rationale already labels output a forecast. Adding an nvfp4/mxfp4 key and a 'forecast, verify with real run' provenance note is surgical and keeps the estimate honest-by-label. Plan rather than ship-now: re-anchoring constants without a real measurement just swaps one unverified guess for another, so do it alongside any real benchmark data and keep the numbers defensible. ASCII only, never the forbidden word.

verifiedProposals.title: Reconcile runtimes.html 'phone best fit' with foundation-only mobile status
verifiedProposals.reason: Verified contradiction: runtimes.html marks 'phone (on-device)' is-best ('Best fit at this volume', $0/mo, 96 ms) and sells Core ML/LiteRT/ExecuTorch device edge, while devices.js PROFILES are runtime_status:'foundation' with 'No iOS/Android runtime ships yet'. verifiedProposals.title: Surface a 'blocked reason' contract assertion in placement output
verifiedProposals.reason: Partly real: placementDecision already emits a human-readable rationale and meetsRequirement already returns machine-readable reason codes (insufficient_vram, no_compatible_runtime, missing_modality, no_confidential_compute, attestation_mismatch). The gap is a single canonical machine-readable blocked_reason field to match the runtimes.html 'Blocked, with the reason' promise. Buildable and small, but it spans two modules with overlapping shapes - normalize them together rather than bolt on a third. Plan.

verifiedProposals.title: Runtime/Device Readiness Passport is unsigned and not in the transparency log
verifiedProposals.reason: Accurate gap: runtime-passport.js produces tested/estimated/unsupported rows with no Ed25519 signature and no Merkle inclusion, while surface A signs+logs reports - so surface B's where/how-fast claims are unverifiable assertions, contradicting the whole offline-evidence wedge. verifiedProposals.title: Placement decision never reaches the live run path
verifiedProposals.reason: The observation is literally true (runtime-placement.js carries TODO(future-wave-runtime) and is pure; runtime.js does not call placementDecision). But the implied fix is mis-targeted: runtime.js getCompiled() loads a JS/WASM concept function, not model weights, so there is nothing here to enforce placement on. The marketing claim 'smallest runtime that fits' is served by the export/recommend path and external runtimes, not by an in-process model loader that does not exist. Reject as written; the honest framing is 'no in-process model run path exists', which is a roadmap item, not a wiring fix.

verifiedProposals.title: Placement free memory is a static boot-time guess, never refreshed
verifiedProposals.reason: Verified: detectMemoryHierarchy sets free_gb = vram_gb*0.95 at boot, comment admits it should be refreshed, no refresh path exists; on a contended box placementDecision can pick full_gpu that would OOM. Real and buildable (improvement #7). Plan, not urgent, because no live loader currently consumes the decision, so the OOM scenario it prevents is not reachable in this stack today.

But closing it requires a real inference path emitting perf_sample events, which this Node monolith does not have (runtime.js runs concept functions).
