currentState:
The serving-inference-gateway is a deep, internally-consistent layer with two distinct halves grounded in the 14 files read.

SERVE/AUTODETECT (surface-A producers): serve-config.js (1341 lines) is a fully deterministic, pure-function serve-config picker: parseComputeCapability/kernelCapabilityForCC gate quantized-GEMM kernels by SM capability (Marlin, Machete sm_90a, MoE-Marlin, W4A8, FP8 W8A8, NVFP4 W4A4 sm_100) with fallback chains; resolveServingKernel/selectKvCachePolicy (StreamingLLM/H2O/SnapKV/PyramidKV/KIVI/Shard) and emitKvPolicyVllmConfig; resolveEagleHead + buildVllmSpeculativeConfig/buildSglangSpecArgs/buildLlamaCppDraftArgs; resolveServingFeatures + emitVllmServeArgs/emitSglangServeArgs; planMultiLora/estimateAdapterPoolVram. Each picker emits a *passportEntry (provenance for surface B). serve-autodetect.js (628) selects among llama.cpp/vllm/mlx/ollama and emits docker-compose + K8s Deployment/Service/HPA/ConfigMap/PVC. serve-metrics-sidecar.js (153) exposes Prometheus /metrics + /health parsing runtime stdout.

GATEWAY/ROUTE (runtime): gateway-router.js (381) circuit-breaker-aware dispatchWithFallback that never skips the last candidate; gateway-mode.js (407) local Ollama/vLLM/mock dispatch; gateway-guardrail.js (466) inline injection guardrail (off/detect_only/flag/block, threshold 0.85, never throws, additive non-signed receipt.guardrail field); gateway-receipt.js (209) builds+signs the 19-field kolm-audit-1 receipt (Ed25519, key-revocation-aware verify). Routers: confidence-router.js (420, O(1) streaming entropy window + weakness-signal feedback to W720), semantic-router.js (1039, k-means cluster router + multi-signal blend, default OFF), ab-router.js (1097, fnv1a deterministic split + Welch t-test + anytime-valid mSPRT/GAVI + auto-rollback), route-quality-store.js (491, closes the semantic cold-start loop). MCP: mcp-gateway.js (380) signs mcp-tool-call-1 receipts binding {tool,args_hash,result_hash,tenant,ts}; mcp-gateway-routes.js (242) mounts /v1/mcp/dispatch + /v1/mcp/verify. inference-bench.js (556) signs reproducible baseline-vs-speculative benchmark rows.

The signed-receipt provenance moat is real and differentiated. The principal weaknesses are seams that are present but unwired or inconsistent: guardrail transformer tier (Prompt-Guard-2) is a seam not on by default; gateway-mode local dispatch path produces NO signed receipt; serving/MCP receipts are not anchored to the advertised RFC-6962 Merkle transparency log; metrics expose only p50 (no TTFT/p95/p99/error/queue/GPU); HPA scales on CPU util; two divergent speculative resolvers and a truncated-vs-full sha256 inconsistency between receipt modules create drift risk.
sota: 2026 frontier scan. SERVING OBSERVABILITY: the production baseline is now TTFT-p99, inter-token latency (ITL), request queue depth, KV-cache utilization, GPU util/mem, and aggregate tok/s, exported via Prometheus with OpenTelemetry distributed tracing end-to-end (client->gateway->engine); kolm's sidecar surfaces only p50 latency + p50 tok/s, materially behind (sitepoint vLLM 2026 guide; parseable vLLM OTel; runpod serving architecture). ROUTING: vLLM Semantic Router "Iris" v0.1 (Jan 2026) moves routing BELOW the app layer using a single ModernBERT LoRA multi-task classifier; Avengers-Pro (DAI 2025) is training-free embed+cluster+performance-efficiency-score routing with a tunable alpha; the 2026 shift is bandit-feedback online routers (BaRP, PILOT) replacing static classifiers (github vllm-project/semantic-router; arxiv 2508.12631; zylos.ai routing). kolm's semantic-router (k-means + multi-signal blend + route-quality feedback) is at parity with Avengers-Pro and ahead of static classifiers, but lacks the bandit/online-update closed loop and ships default-OFF. MCP GATEWAY: signed tamper-evident tool-call receipts are now an emerging category (protect-mcp Ed25519 hash-chained W3C-VC receipts; Bifrost MCP audit logs at ~11us/req); kolm's signed mcp-tool-call-1 receipt is competitive, but competitors hash-CHAIN receipts whereas kolm's are individually signed and NOT yet Merkle-anchored (getmaxim Bifrost; kya.vouched.id; dev.to signed receipt). GUARDRAILS: Prompt-Guard-2 (86M/22M BERT) inside LlamaFirewall is the de-facto inline injection scanner; kolm has the exact seam but heuristic-only by default (meta-llama PurpleLlama Prompt-Guard-2; getmaxim injection-defense 2026). A/B: anytime-valid confidence sequences (mSPRT, AsympCS) are the calibrated industry standard (Adobe Experience Platform; arxiv 2302.10108) - kolm's ab-router already implements mSPRT/GAVI, at SOTA.

verifiedProposals.title: Merkle-anchored unified receipt spine for every dispatch (local, frontier, MCP)
verifiedProposals.reason: Verified: src/merkle.js already implements RFC 6962/9162 buildTree/proof/verifyInclusion but neither gateway-receipt.js nor mcp-gateway.js imports it, and both verifiers are signature-only. Wiring an append+inclusion-proof spine is the single highest-leverage moat move here; Ed25519+SHA-256 only, no blockchain. Larger than surgical (needs a persisted append-only log store + proof surfacing on two verify routes), so plan.

verifiedProposals.title: Signed serving-SLO evidence: TTFT/ITL/p99 receipts derived from token-stream timing
verifiedProposals.reason: Grounded: signResult (ed25519) exists in inference-bench.js to reuse. But the sidecar only ingests per-completion log lines (parseRuntimeLogLine), it has NO per-token timestamp stream, so TTFT/ITL require a real streaming hook that does not exist yet - net-new plumbing, not a tweak. The signed SLO artifact is an evidence record, not a warranty, so it does not collide with the locked scope sentence. Worth building; plan.

verifiedProposals.title: Online bandit overlay on the semantic router with signed exploration ledger
verifiedProposals.reason: Real seams exist (recordRouteOutcome/trainRouteWeights in route-quality-store.js, route_mode flag + multi-signal scoreRoute in semantic-router.js, confidence-router weakness signals). Buildable behind a default-static flag. But online bandit posteriors per tenant/cluster plus a signed event ledger is a sizable, easy-to-get-wrong addition with lower direct revenue tie-in than the receipt/SLO work; plan, not now.

verifiedProposals.title: Single source of truth for speculative + serve config with a signed config digest
verifiedProposals.reason: Confirmed drift: serve-config.resolveEagleHead (3-entry EAGLE_HEAD_REGISTRY) and speculative-decoding.resolveSpeculative (used by inference-bench) are independent resolvers that can disagree. Collapsing to one resolver + a signed config_digest the bench verifies is sound and reuses signResult. Effort is small-to-medium but touches the bench contract and two serve arg builders; plan to avoid a hasty provenance change.

verifiedProposals.title: Wire Prompt-Guard-2 transformer tier into the default guardrail path with heuristic prefilter
verifiedProposals.reason: Accurate: classifyTransformer is a real out-of-process seam never invoked on the default path; applyGuardrail keeps detect_only + heuristic-only. Taking max(heuristic, transformer) while fail-open is the right design. But it depends on an external Prompt-Guard-2 sidecar endpoint that is not part of this stack today, so 'default path' wiring is gated on standing up that service - plan, and keep detect_only the unconfigured default to honor 'injection tested and reported, not warranted'.

verifiedProposals.title: Add TTFT and p95/p99 gauges to the metrics sidecar
verifiedProposals.reason: Verified gap: renderPrometheus emits only p50 latency, p50 tok/s, request_count, memory_mb, uptime; a percentile() helper already exists. Adding p95/p99 gauges and an error counter is a surgical, low-risk edit to one file. TTFT cannot be derived from existing log lines (no first-token field), so ship the percentile/error gauges now and treat TTFT as the streaming follow-up.

verifiedProposals.title: Emit signed receipts on gateway-mode local/vLLM dispatch
verifiedProposals.reason: Confirmed: dispatchByMode/localOllamaCall/localVllmCall return raw envelopes with zero calls to gateway-receipt.buildAndSignReceipt, so the local route has no provenance while cloud does. buildAndSignReceipt already supports route_decision='local' + artifact_id. Medium because gateway-mode.js is deliberately the leak-proof chokepoint and currently imports no receipt module; adding signing there must preserve the honest-envelope/no-throw contract. Strong value; plan.

verifiedProposals.title: Unify sha256 width across receipt modules
verifiedProposals.reason: Real inconsistency: gateway-receipt._sha256Short truncates to 32 hex for input_hash/output_hash while mcp-gateway hashMcpArgs/hashMcpResult use full 64 hex. Worth reconciling for verifier clarity, but the 32-hex width is baked into already-issued kolm-audit-1 receipts and the receipt schema; widening it is a breaking change to historical verification, so this is a documented-canonical-width decision, not a free edit. Plan.

verifiedProposals.title: Switch the K8s HPA to LLM-appropriate signals
verifiedProposals.reason: Verified: buildK8sManifests emits an autoscaling/v2 HPA on cpu averageUtilization 75. CPU is the wrong signal for token serving. But a queue-depth/KV-cache/GPU custom-metric HPA requires a metrics-adapter (Prometheus Adapter or KEDA) the emitter cannot assume exists, so a naive Pods/External metric YAML would reference a metric nothing publishes and break apply. Plan: emit GPU/custom-metric target with a documented adapter prerequisite and CPU fallback.

verifiedProposals.title: Rename internal Honest/Honesty code comments to Caveat/Constraint
verifiedProposals.reason: Confirmed: serve-config.js line 50 ('Honest gates') and line 685 ('honest vLLM enforcement') use the forbidden word family. These are internal comments (not a customer/API surface) so the hard constraint is not technically violated today, but renaming them is a zero-risk one-file edit that removes any chance of leakage into a generated doc/passport string. Low value, trivial; fine to ship.

verifiedProposals.title: Local/MCP dispatch paths produce no signed receipt; serving + MCP receipts are not Merkle-anchored to the transparency log
verifiedProposals.reason: Fully verified against code: gateway-mode.js never signs; mcp-gateway-routes signs an mcp-tool-call-1 receipt but never appends to merkle.js; gateway-receipt.verifyReceipt is signature+revocation only with no inclusion-proof. This is the real, central provenance gap behind the top inventions - accurate and worth fixing; plan.

verifiedProposals.title: Metrics sidecar is below the 2026 serving-SLO baseline: no TTFT, no p95/p99, no error/queue/GPU
verifiedProposals.reason: Confirmed: renderPrometheus surfaces p50 latency/tok-s, request_count, linux-only memory_mb, uptime - no TTFT, p95/p99, errors, queue, or GPU; parseRuntimeLogLine extracts only total tokens + wall latency. The p95/p99/error subset is shippable now; TTFT/queue/GPU need new ingestion. Accurate gap framing; plan the full closure.

verifiedProposals.title: Two divergent speculative-decoding resolvers can silently disagree
verifiedProposals.reason: Verified: serve-config.js resolveEagleHead builds serve spec args from a 3-entry EAGLE_HEAD_REGISTRY, while inference-bench.js imports a separate resolveSpeculative from speculative-decoding.js for its benchmark arms. Two independent resolvers => a signed bench config can differ from the deployed serve config, a genuine provenance hole in a signed-benchmark product. Real; pairs with the config-digest invention.

verifiedProposals.title: Injection guardrail defaults to heuristic-only detect_only; transformer tier present but unwired
verifiedProposals.reason: Accurate: applyGuardrail default mode is detect_only over classifyHeuristic regex at DEFAULT_THRESHOLD 0.85; classifyTransformer is a real but uninvoked seam. Regex recall on obfuscated/multilingual injections is low. Note the scope sentence already says injection is 'tested and reported, not warranted', so detect_only is defensible as a stated limitation - the fix raises recall without overclaiming. Real gap; plan.

verifiedProposals.title: Truncated (32-hex) vs full (64-hex) sha256 inconsistency across receipt modules
verifiedProposals.reason: Verified: gateway-receipt._sha256Short emits sha256:<32 hex>; mcp-gateway _hashCanonical emits sha256:<64 hex>. The mismatch is real and can confuse a verifier picking a width, but 32-hex is already in issued kolm-audit-1 receipts so reconciliation is a versioned/documented decision rather than a silent widen. Real but lower severity; plan.

verifiedProposals.title: HPA scales LLM serving on CPU averageUtilization only
verifiedProposals.reason: Confirmed at serve-autodetect.js buildK8sManifests (Resource cpu, averageUtilization 75). CPU is the wrong autoscale signal for token generation. Genuine gap, but closing it correctly depends on a custom-metrics adapter outside this stack, so it is plan-with-prerequisite, not an immediate emit. Low priority vs the provenance work.

verifiedProposals.title: Semantic router ships default-OFF and lacks a bandit/online-update loop
verifiedProposals.reason: Accurate: semantic-router defaults route_mode='static'; route-quality-store trainRouteWeights is batch/offline; recordRouteOutcome + confidence-router weakness signals exist but are not wired into a live exploration policy. Correct description of the state and the frontier delta. It is a capability gap, not a defect, and lower-revenue than the evidence-spine items; plan.

currentState: This component is the deepest and most architecturally coherent surface I reviewed on the kolm stack, but it has a structural contradiction with kolm's own trust positioning. What exists and works: (1) A dual-registry model catalog (src/model-registry.js, 1549 lines) with FRONTIER_MODELS (verified) vs CANDIDATE_MODELS (watchlist) vs BACKBONES, a strict W295 verification gate (EXACT_HF_MODEL_URL=/^https:\/\/huggingface\.co\/[^/]+\/[^/]+\/?$/ specificity check, verifyEntry/verifyAll, and online HEAD probing via verifyEntryOnline with a 10s abort budget and source_url_specific surfacing). resolveTier reads ONLY verified rows and returns a non-null reason payload when no verified model targets a tier (good amber-pill discipline). detectTierFromGpuName/detectBackendFromGpuName cover NVIDIA/AMD/Apple. (2) Device-aware fit math in src/models.js (fitsOn/explainFit, mobile pick google/gemma-3n-E2B-it, DEFAULT_MODEL Qwen/Qwen2.5-3B-Instruct, pins at .kolm/model-pins.json). (3) Export to kolm/github/huggingface/ollama/custom (src/model-export.js) with an SSRF guard (assertPublicHttpsUrl) and tokens-held-in-closure-never-persisted. (4) Merge engine (src/model-merge.js) with frozen TIES/DARE/DELLA/SLERP/linear methods, same-base hard gate, delta-W+SVD, and lineage binding through a Python worker with a durable no-tool envelope. (5) HF Model Card v0.3 emission (src/model-card-emit.js, MODEL_CARD_VERSION 'w768-v1') with a draft-07 JSON schema and governance-platform mappings to OneTrust/ServiceNow/IBM OpenPages (src/model-card-schema.js). (6) Pure-metadata weights manifest + resumable node:https streaming puller (src/model-weights-manifest.js, src/model-weights-puller.js) that deliberately avoids global fetch on Windows (W305/W348). (7) Tenant-fenced model entitlements with chargeback namespaces (src/model-entitlements.js). (8) ASR-5 supply-chain provenance analyzer (src/model-provenance-analyzer.js) flagging unpinned-model-version/opaque-model-routing/unpinned-mcp-server/model-egress-third-party, mapped to OWASP LLM03 / MITRE ATLAS / NIST / ISO. (9) Provider layer: 11-adapter registry with 2026 cost tables (src/provider-registry.js), AES-256-GCM encrypted provider-key vault (src/provider-vault.js), and a 3-state circuit-breaker health registry with fail-open panic mode (src/provider-health.js). (10) Deterministic NL assistant + three-layer fallback client with per-turn cost cap (src/assistant.js, src/assistant-client.js). The core weakness: despite kolm selling cryptographic, offline-verifiable trust (Ed25519 fa56..., Merkle log), this entire model surface signs nothing. revision_hash is ALWAYS null and revision_pinned:false across the registry; the weights puller only verifies sha256 when file.sha256 is provided, and it is never provided (manifest rows have no hardcoded digests); exported artifacts and emitted model cards carry no signature; pullBackbone sets pull_status='pulled_and_verified' on the basis of non-empty bytes alone (size>0), not a digest match -- so "verified" overstates what was checked. Secondary issues: provider-vault PROVIDERS lists mistral+cohere that have no adapters in provider-registry (capability mismatch); the kolm-native byte-BPE tokenizer (src/tokenizer.js) cannot reproduce real model token counts so any cost/context math derived from it is approximate; chat templates (src/chat-templates.js) are missing for several registered families (Gemma/Mistral/Yi/GLM); the registry carries speculative 2026 candidate rows and the weights manifest FALLBACKs to "closest real model"; and the literal word "HONESTY CONTRACT" sits in src/model-card-emit.js comments (a forbidden-word liability if it ever reaches a card surface).
sota: The 2026 frontier for this exact domain has consolidated around cryptographic model provenance, which is precisely where kolm under-delivers relative to its own trust story. (1) Model signing is now a shipped standard: the OpenSSF sigstore/model-transparency project + model-signing PyPI package wrap a signed manifest of per-file digests in a DSSE envelope carrying an in-toto statement (who signed, from what pipeline, when), and the OpenSSF model-signing-spec (github.com/ossf/model-signing-spec) is the emerging interop format; Google and Red Hat have public case studies of keyless Sigstore signing over whole model artifacts (blog.sigstore.dev/model-transparency-v1.0). kolm cannot use keyless Sigstore (Fulcio CA = external request, and Rekor is a non-kolm transparency log), but the in-toto statement + DSSE + per-file Merkle manifest pattern is directly replicable with kolm's own Ed25519 key and existing Merkle log -- this is a moat, not a constraint. (2) The "Tampered Tokenizers" research (control-plane.io) shows tokenizer files are an under-guarded supply-chain attack vector -- directly relevant since kolm ships its own tokenizer.json referenced by sha256; kolm already hashes it (good) but does not sign it or pin it inside a signed artifact manifest. (3) AI/ML-BOM is now real: CycloneDX 1.5+ ships a Machine-Learning-BOM (cyclonedx.org/capabilities/mlbom) and SPDX 3.0 added AI-specific fields and is heading to ISO/IEC 5962 update; multiple 2026 papers (AIBoMGen arxiv 2601.05703, Open AIBOM arxiv 2510.07070) operationalize signed AIBOMs. kolm emits model cards but no machine-readable, signed ML-BOM. (4) OWASP LLM03:2025 Supply Chain (genai.owasp.org) explicitly recommends "pin model checkpoints to a verified hash, track provenance under SLSA + SBOM," and notes provenance "cannot be reliably assessed through keyword matching alone" -- which is both validation of kolm's ASR-5 analyzer existing and an indictment that the analyzer flags unpinned models while kolm's own registry ships every model unpinned. SLSA-style build provenance for the merge/distill pipeline is the natural next layer. Net: the building blocks kolm already owns (Ed25519, SHA-256 Merkle, per-file hashing) put it one focused wave away from category-best signed provenance; today it talks the trust story without applying it to its own model artifacts.

verifiedProposals.title: Signed Model Provenance Bundle (.kolm model attestation) -- in-toto/DSSE pattern over per-file Merkle using existing Ed25519 + Merkle log
verifiedProposals.reason: Infra is real and reusable: ed25519.js (sign/verify/keyFingerprint/loadOrCreateDefaultSigner), merkle.js (RFC6962 leafHash/computeRoot/inclusionPath/verifyInclusion), transparency-log.js, and public/kolm-audit-verify.js all exist. Directly closes the stack's own 'every artifact signed + provenance-tracked' promise and unifies surface B with the surface A moat. No blockchain, no external CA, no new fonts/CDNs. L effort (new module + export/merge wiring + verifier extension), so plan not ship-now. Watch the FORBIDDEN '.kolm bundle' substring -- name the file artifact.attestation.json, never '.kolm bundle'.

verifiedProposals.title: Real revision pinning + digest-gated 'verified' -- close ASR-5
verifiedProposals.reason: Grounded: revision_hash is always null / revision_pinned:false across FRONTIER_MODELS, and the puller only verifies sha256 when file.sha256 is set (manifest never sets it). pullFile already computes sha256 on full downloads, so a hard gate is a natural extension. The dogfooding angle (platform satisfies its own ASR-5) is a genuine differentiator. M effort touching registry + puller + provenance-analyzer; pin file should NOT be named '.kolm bundle'. Pairs with the ship-now rename below.

verifiedProposals.title: Signed ML-BOM (CycloneDX-shaped) emitted alongside every model card
verifiedProposals.reason: Buildable: tokenizer.sha256() exists and chat-templates manifestBlock() emits integrity_hash, so the inputs are real; card emitter (buildModelCard) is the right hook and already targets governance platforms. Depends on the model-attestation invention for signing, so sequence after it. Lower priority than the two pinning/signing items because it is additive packaging, not a trust-gap fix. Keep ASCII-only (no em/en dashes) in emitted strings -- the existing emitter already uses ASCII hyphens, preserve that.

verifiedProposals.title: Tokenizer-tamper guard: pin tokenizer + chat-template digests inside the signed manifest
verifiedProposals.reason: Real hooks: tokenizer SPEC + sha256 and chat-templates manifestBlock().integrity_hash both exist and are recomputable for compare-at-load. Sound defense, but it is a sub-feature of the model-attestation invention (it just adds two named subjects + a verify step), so it cannot ship independently and should fold into that workstream rather than stand as its own M-effort item. Med impact; verify-before-serve path must be wired into the serving gateway, which is the larger cost.

verifiedProposals.title: Stop labeling downloads 'verified' without a digest match
verifiedProposals.reason: Exactly correct against code: model-registry pullBackbone sets pulled_and_verified on (fixtureBytes||useRealPuller) && not-backbone.json && size>0 (lines 1194-1199); verifyBackbone (1234-1238) only checks existence + size. No digest anywhere. For a product whose pitch is cryptographic verification this is the single most dangerous overstatement and the fix is surgical: gate the 'verified' state on an actual sha256 match, else emit 'pulled'/'pulled_unverified'. High value, S effort, safe.

verifiedProposals.title: Remove the forbidden 'HONESTY CONTRACT' string from the model-card emitter
verifiedProposals.reason: Partially mis-framed. 'HONESTY CONTRACT' and 'honest' appear ONLY in code comments and the internal variable name HONEST_NOT_DISCLOSED; the emitted card output is the sentinel 'not_yet_disclosed' and never contains 'honest'. So the claim that a model card is 'one refactor away from surfacing' it is speculative -- there is NO current customer/API-surface violation, hence not a hard-constraint breach. It is still defensible hygiene because the binding memory says avoid honest/honesty 'anywhere'; rename the comment + the HONEST_NOT_DISCLOSED constant. Low priority, do it opportunistically, not as an urgent fix.

verifiedProposals.title: Reconcile provider-vault PROVIDERS with provider-registry adapters
verifiedProposals.reason: Confirmed dead-key UX: provider-vault PROVIDERS (line 26) lists 'mistral' and 'cohere' but provider-registry has no _ADAPTER_LOADERS entry, no PROVIDERS config block, and they are absent from SUPPORTED_PROVIDER_IDS -- a stored key can never route. Safest surgical fix is to remove the two from the vault list and have putProviderKey reject providers not in the gateway's resolvable set. S effort. Note: openai/anthropic also lack adapter loaders but ARE configured + supported (routed differently), so do NOT strip those.

verifiedProposals.title: Surface 'token_count_approximate' caveat wherever kolm tokenizer counts feed cost/context math
verifiedProposals.reason: Real basis: tokenizer.js header (lines 16-19) states it is NOT a HuggingFace tokenizer, so counts diverge from real model tokenizers, and provider-registry carries cost_per_1k tables that consumers multiply. Honest disclosure is on-brand and uses 'approximate'/'estimate' (not the forbidden word family). Effort is touching several surfaces consistently; verify the kolm tokenizer actually feeds the specific cost/fit paths named before wiring flags, since provider cost_per_1k is priced per-provider-model and may not always route through the kolm tokenizer.

verifiedProposals.title: Add chat templates for Gemma/Mistral/Yi/GLM; make pickTemplate fail loud on unknown family
verifiedProposals.reason: Confirmed: TEMPLATES covers chatml/qwen-3-thinking/llama-3/phi-3/deepseek-v4/plain/kolm-think only; Gemma, Mistral, Yi, GLM have no match and pickTemplate silently returns 'plain', which mis-prompts. Mobile default in models.js is gemma-3n-E2B-it, so a wrong-template path is on the hot path. Add real family templates (Gemma turn markers at minimum) and add a 'no_template_for_family' signal. M effort because each template needs correct marker bytes; do not just rename plain. Correctness fix worth doing soon.

verifiedProposals.title: Flag substitute weight rows so 'pull' never silently returns a different model's bytes
verifiedProposals.reason: Grounded: model-weights-manifest ALL_VARIANTS maps unshipped ids (Qwen3.6-27B, GLM-4.7-Flash, Kimi-K2.5-1T, Gemma-3-27B, etc.) to a 'closest real model' GGUF, disclosed only in free-text notes. Adding is_substitute / substitutes_for to the row and propagating it into pullVariant's result + receipt is small, safe, and high-credibility for a trust product. S effort, no constraint risk. Surfacing the existing notes string into a structured flag is the right minimal move.

verifiedProposals.title: Record DNS-rebind as a named limitation in the export receipt
verifiedProposals.reason: Accurate: model-export assertPublicHttpsUrl (lines 102-103) concedes DNS-rebind is out of scope in a comment only. Promoting it to an explicit 'limitations' line on the custom-destination receipt is trivial, on-brand disclosure using allowed wording ('Limitations'), and surgical. Low impact but near-zero effort and zero risk, so ship it alongside other receipt touches.

verifiedProposals.title: GAP: 'pulled_and_verified' asserted on byte-count, not a digest match
verifiedProposals.reason: Verified verbatim in model-registry.js (1194-1199 verifiedWeights; 1234-1238 verifyBackbone) and model-weights-puller.js (sha256 only when file.sha256 provided, which the manifest never sets). This is the correct identification of the top trust-integrity gap on this surface. Same fix as the ship-now rename improvement; do them together.

verifiedProposals.title: GAP: No model artifact is cryptographically signed, contradicting the signed-artifact promise
verifiedProposals.reason: Accurate: model-export pushes to 5 destinations with no signing, buildModelCard emits no signature block, model-merge binds lineage but does not sign. The Ed25519 key + Merkle log + public verifier exist and are unused here. Real, high-value gap; remediation is the L-effort model-attestation invention, hence plan. ASR-5 dogfooding framing is fair.

verifiedProposals.title: GAP: Registry revision pinning permanently disabled (revision_hash null everywhere)
verifiedProposals.reason: Confirmed: every FRONTIER_MODELS row has revision_hash:null + revision_pinned:false, and verifyEntryOnline only HEADs source_url (liveness), not byte identity -- a genuine TOCTOU gap for a security-evidence product. Pairs with the pinning invention. Plan, not ship-now, because real pinning needs network-sourced SHAs the codebase deliberately does not fabricate offline.

verifiedProposals.title: GAP: Provider capability mismatch -- vault advertises providers with no routing adapter
verifiedProposals.reason: Correct: provider-vault PROVIDERS includes 'mistral' and 'cohere' that have no _ADAPTER_LOADERS entry and are not in SUPPORTED_PROVIDER_IDS -- encryptable but unroutable keys. Same surgical fix as the reconcile improvement. Caveat for the implementer: openai/anthropic also have no loader but are configured/supported, so the dead set is specifically mistral+cohere.

verifiedProposals.title: GAP: kolm-native tokenizer cannot measure real model token counts; cost/fit math unlabeled
verifiedProposals.reason: Real per tokenizer.js header (16-19): byte-BPE, no HF regex pretok / normalization / added tokens, so counts drift from Qwen/Llama/DeepSeek. Disclosure is the right remedy. Plan because the fix is the multi-surface caveat improvement above and the implementer must first confirm which cost/fit paths actually consume the kolm tokenizer vs provider-published per-model pricing.

verifiedProposals.title: GAP: Chat templates missing for several registered families
verifiedProposals.reason: Confirmed: registry/manifest register Gemma (default mobile pick gemma-3n-E2B-it), Mistral, GLM, etc., none of which match TEMPLATES, so pickTemplate falls back to 'plain' and mis-formats. (Yi is not actually in the registry rows read, but Gemma/Mistral/GLM are -- core claim holds.) Same remediation as the templates improvement; correctness gap worth fixing soon.

verifiedProposals.title: GAP: Weights manifest FALLBACKs to 'closest real model' + speculative candidate rows -- provenance ambiguity
verifiedProposals.reason: Accurate: ALL_VARIANTS substitutes unshipped ids to closest GGUF (disclosed only in notes), and CANDIDATE_MODELS holds speculative 2026 rows. Without a structured is_substitute flag a pull can return different bytes than the id implies. Same small, safe fix as the substitute-flag improvement; high-credibility for a trust product.

verifiedProposals.title: GAP: SSRF guard documents DNS-rebind as out of scope
verifiedProposals.reason: Confirmed in model-export.js (102-103): private/loopback/link-local/reserved ranges are blocked but DNS-rebind is explicitly out of scope in a comment. Correctly scoped as acceptable-for-now; the right move is the receipt 'Limitations' disclosure (allowed wording). Low impact, trivial effort.

currentState:
This is the most mature engineering surface in the stack and the backbone of the "prove model quality" claim. K-Score (src/kscore.js, 391 lines) is a real composite: V1_WEIGHTS={A:0.40,S:0.15,L:0.15,C:0.15,V:0.15}, a V2 extension adding R/T/F/E/Z axes with documented weight redistribution when an axis is absent, GATE=0.85, MIN_HONEST_A_FOR_RATIO=0.05, and a W714 opt-in contrastive axis. W810 external calibration is genuinely strong: _attachW810Calibration maps K-Score to human_preference_rate via a Bradley-Terry latent-skill fit (src/bradley-terry.js, 265 lines, quasi-Newton + ridge=1e-3 + Rao-Kupper ties) plus logit bin-and-fit (src/kscore-calibration.js, 427 lines) with Wald CI95, MIN_PAIRS_PER_CATEGORY=500, CALIBRATION_CATEGORIES=[coding,writing,analysis,support], and a strict contract that returns statuses (no_calibration_mapping / insufficient_data / unknown_category) and never falls back to a pooled mapping. Per-language (kscore-per-language.js, Wilson CI, n>=30) and time-series (kscore-timeseries.js, kts-v1 event-store-backed, idempotent backfill, trend/regression summary) layers exist.

The bench harness (src/bench-harness.js, 1080 lines) is multi-transport with a heuristic defaultJudge, dry-run, a markdown Caveats section, and an inline cost table. bench-eval-suites.js (379 lines) defines 4 hardcoded suites with a closed METRIC_REGISTRY and validateSuite. bakeoff.js (635 lines) ranks by score_per_dollar with parse_failure_rate + context_faithfulness axes and a closed recommendationVerdict enum, but its default correctness check is crude Jaccard pass/fail. Specialized bakeoffs exist: adversarial-bakeoff.js (W762, heuristic refusal-keyword judge tagged judge_kind:'heuristic'), quantize-bakeoff.js (W719-3 Pareto over kscore x vram where scoreJaccard is an explicit SURROGATE = avgBits/8 + coverage unless KOLM_BAKEOFF_SCORE_CMD is wired), and redaction-benchmark.js (precision/recall/f1 vs redactPhi on a synthetic fixture, minF1=0.95). KolmBench (kolmbench.js, 399 lines, W756 v1, 50 AUTHORITATIVE_TASKS) ships but scoreSubmission returns null k_score with reason 'kolmbench_v1_scoring_offline_pending_pack' and appendLeaderboardEntry sets verified:false absent an Ed25519 receipt -- auto-scoring is NOT shipped (blocked_by W757/W766). The eval-* family (eval-humaneval.js, eval-mmlu.js, eval-mtbench.js) are honest scaffolds that refuse to score without a local pack + sandbox/judge (bench_pack_not_local / no_code_sandbox_configured / no_judge_model_configured); eval-numeric.js delegates to a safe arithmetic evaluator (no eval()). external-holdout.js (303 lines) requires expected_sha256 and binds it into artifact_hash for tamper-evidence; holdouts/catalog.json carries 3 CC0 synthetic corpora (~40 rows each). benchmark-evidence.js (430 lines) is a readiness auditor over 7 provider lanes with a secret-value guard and hashes-only contract. bandit-thompson.js (658 lines, W921) is a full Normal-Gamma Thompson sampler (discounted gamma=0.9, cost-aware, warm-start, never throws). The defining pattern throughout is the refuse-rather-than-fabricate envelope.
sota: 2026 frontier for "prove model quality" has moved decisively toward contamination-resistant and continuously-renewed benchmarks: LiveCodeBench and LiveBench partition problems by release date so any item predating a model's training cutoff is excluded; FrontierMath and MMLU-Pro raise ceiling + reduce saturation. Contamination detection is now expected tooling: Min-K%-Prob and Min-K%++ (membership inference on token log-probs), ConStat (statistical contamination test comparing performance on a reference vs suspected-leaked set), and canary GUID strings -- with the documented caveat that public canaries can backfire (models trained to recognize/avoid them, or filtered out, biasing estimates). LLM-as-judge work in 2026 emphasizes calibration correction: report judge sensitivity/specificity and apply Rogan-Gladen prevalence correction, surface length-controlled win rates (AlpacaEval-LC lineage) as the primary ship metric, and treat overlapping 95% CIs among top models as "statistically tied" rather than ranking on point estimates. Chatbot-Arena-style Bradley-Terry with bootstrap CIs is the de-facto external-validity anchor (kolm's W810 already implements the BT half well). IRT-based item discrimination/difficulty indices are increasingly used to prune weak items and detect saturation. Net: kolm's calibration + envelope discipline are at or above many vendors, but the SUITE LAYER (frozen in-repo prompts, no freshness/renewal, no contamination check) and the JUDGE-CI SURFACING lag the 2026 bar.

currentState: Seven subsystems read end-to-end. CONCEPT REGISTRY (src/registry.js): versioned concept store with per-version embeddings, semver autogen, lineage (synthesized_from/dependencies), tenant+team+public ACL, HTML/URL-safe sanitization for public /v1/registry/export. searchSimilar() embeds query then LINEAR cosine scan over every readable head vector (no ANN index), skipping rows lacking numeric vectors (W470). MODEL REGISTRY (src/model-registry.js, 1549 lines): FRONTIER_MODELS (10 verified rows, exact HF model-card URL regex + exact license_url + verified_at; revision_hash honestly null/unpinned), CANDIDATE_MODELS (org-URL watchlist, amber pill, never used by resolveTier), BACKBONES (pull_status registered|metadata_cached|pulled_and_verified, empty recommended_for_target until real device smoke test). verifyExactSourceUrl single source of truth with lookalike-host guard. MOE REGISTRY: 7 frozen family topology records + ARCH_TO_FAMILY priority lookup; hint-only, defers to config.json. MARKETPLACE = THREE OVERLAPPING LAYERS: (1) W263/W342 curated seed catalog (marketplace.js): 8 .kolm seeds, each must resolve to real bytes or is dropped; live sha256+bytes; k_score from registry-pack/bench/gate; verified FORCED false at sync layer, only re-promoted by router/CLI overlay via LIVE async productionReady() (W428 lock-in); getCatalogManifest signs sha256 anchor + Ed25519 sidecar; installArtifactFromBytes re-runs productionReady() against downloaded bytes + sha256 check. (2) W737 publisher store (marketplace-store.js): event-store registerArtifact/listArtifactsForBrowse, submitReview/getReviews (NO anti-gaming gate), computeRoyalty 70/30 floor-based. (3) W825 MVP (marketplace-w825.js + -routes/-ratings/-payouts/-finetune): JSONL upsert-by-last-line store, faceted listListings+sort; auth-gated upload with Ed25519 manifest-sig verify + FORCED publisher_tenant_id; paid download 402 entitlement gate + revenue ledger; rate() anti-gaming (account_age>=7d AND prior download); payoutCycle is honest forecast (dispatched:false, no Stripe); finetuneFromMarketplace returns queued envelope (no worker). PLAN CATALOG (plan-catalog.js): single-source SUBSCRIPTION tiers (free/indie/pro/teams/business/enterprise) for /v1/plans + build-time pricing renderer with coherence gate; does NOT hold the LOCKED ASR pricing. TEACHER VERSION (teacher-version.js): stamps teacher_version+teacher_provider per capture (env-resolved, idempotent, never-null fallback), groupByTeacherVersion for drift; default anthropic=claude-opus-4-7. EXPORT FORMAT REGISTRY: metadata-only registry of 8 formats (gguf/exl2/gptq/awq/fp8/nvfp4/hqq/mlx) with quant_levels/runtimes/requires_gpu/install_hint from each export-*.js.
sota: 2026 SOTA for model distribution has converged on signed, transparent, OCI-native artifacts. OpenSSF/Sigstore Model Signing v1.0 (GA 2025) signs models with a DSSE envelope + transparency-log inclusion proof embedding machine-readable model-card + provenance. kolm's Ed25519+SHA-256 Merkle is architecturally aligned and the constraints forbid the Sigstore PKI/blockchain path, so the in-house keypair+Merkle is the correct analog -- but kolm's catalog signature today is ONE Ed25519 sidecar over the whole catalog, NOT a per-artifact inclusion proof in the existing RFC-6962 transparency log that surface A already ships. OCI-artifact packaging (KitOps/Jozu in CNCF Sandbox 150K+ downloads; Docker; Harbor with Cosign/Notary verification, VMware Harbor-as-model-registry; SPDX-3 SBOM + signed provenance attestations) is the de-facto registry substrate; kolm's bespoke .kolm zips are fine for the product but carry no per-artifact SBOM/lineage attestation. Marketplace economics: GPT Store (usage rev-share + editorial review), Claude Skills (free distribution, editorial review), Agensi (80/20 creator split), Cloudflare (inference billing); 2026 storefronts rank by RECENCY of maintenance over raw star count. kolm's 70/30 is publisher-favorable vs the 80/20 high-water mark but in-band. Concept-search SOTA is ANN (HNSW/IVF) not linear cosine scan. Sources: github.com/sigstore/model-transparency; security.googleblog.com/2025/04/taming-wild-west-of-ml; jozu.com; docker.com/blog/oci-artifacts-for-ai-model-packaging; blogs.vmware.com/cloud-foundation/2026/03/03 (Harbor); agensi.io/learn/ai-agent-marketplace-landscape-2026.

currentState: Broad-but-uneven developer reach. The CLI (cli/kolm.js, ~55k bundled lines) is the deep, canonical surface: verb-noun grammar (compile/run/verify/serve/tune/rag), `kolm verify` with --binder/--json/--attestation/--validate-schema/--guardrails, plus a polished TUI (cli/kolm-tui.mjs) with drag-drop .kolm ingest, pure-Node zip reader, and a localhost :serve REST shim (note: TUI run is a deterministic example-matching mock, not real inference). SDKs exist in 8+ targets but with very different depth: Python (sdk/python) and Go (sdk/go) ship the full OFFLINE audit-report Ed25519 verifier with byte-exact JCS-style canonicalization, two-tier verdict (Tier1 signature / Tier2 issuer keyring), and ECMAScript number formatting ported faithfully (verify.go is a strong reference impl). Node SDK (sdk/node/index.mjs) is an HTTP client only - it has NO audit-report verify, only RecipeClient REST calls + captureWithContext. Rust (sdk/rust) and C (sdk/c) verify the .kolm artifact format (manifest CID, HMAC receipt chain) and cover HTTP routes; Swift (packages/sdk-swift) and Kotlin (packages/sdk-kotlin) load+run .kolm on-device with HMAC receipt verify but the actual inference backends are documented stubs that throw. Framework integrations: JS langchain/llamaindex adapters (packages/langchain-kolm, llamaindex-kolm) are real (subprocess + HTTP transport, receipt passthrough) and have package.json; Python langchain/llamaindex adapters exist but lack pyproject/setup so are NOT pip-installable. MCP server (sdk/mcp/server.mjs) is solid - hand-rolled JSON-RPC stdio with 13 recipe tools. VSCode (packages/vscode-kolm-rag) and browser extension (packages/browser-extension, MV3) exist; the browser verifier is candid (does structural zip+sha256 check, explicitly defers full crypto to the CLI rather than faking a green check). Package managers: homebrew/apt/winget manifests all present but homebrew+winget carry placeholder sha256 0000... and point at github.com/kolm-ai/kolm release tarballs that do not exist, so brew/winget install is currently broken. No developer-facing web page surfaces this reach: docs.html section 08 is a 5-line shell snippet, integrations.html is about data connectors not SDKs, and platform/compiler pages only say "no SDK rewrite".
sota: 2026 supply-chain SOTA centers on the in-toto Attestation Framework (ITE-6) envelope as the common interchange, Sigstore (Fulcio short-lived certs + Rekor transparency log + cosign) and SLSA build provenance levels; the June 2026 "Mini Shai-Hulud" npm attack showed that a valid signature/attestation is worthless if issuer provenance and build isolation are not also checked - which validates kolm's Tier1-vs-Tier2 split (signature != trusted issuer). C2PA 2.4 (Feb 2026) standardizes Content Credentials and explicitly lists Ed25519 as an allowed manifest signing alg, so kolm's Ed25519+SHA-256 Merkle path is on-standard; emerging norm is multi-language offline verifiers (cosign/sigstore ship Go+Rust+JS+Python verify) so trust does not depend on one vendor runtime. A2A/agent-card signing discussions push the same "sign the artifact, verify anywhere" pattern kolm is selling. Net: kolm's verify-offline-in-any-language story IS the 2026 differentiator, but only Python+Go currently deliver it - peers ship verify parity across their whole SDK matrix.

verifiedProposals.title: FORBIDDEN substring pip install kolm present in customer-facing surfaces
verifiedProposals.reason: Confirmed at all four cited locations incl. the runtime error print in kolm/__init__.py:243-245. Real FORBIDDEN-list breach. Same fix as improvement #6; ship now.

verifiedProposals.title: Offline audit-report verifier exists in only 2 of 8 SDK languages
verifiedProposals.reason: Verified accurate: only sdk/go/verify.go and sdk/python/audit.py verify the signed audit report; sdk/node is HTTP-only (no verifyReport/ed25519); runtime-rs verify.rs and the kolm-verify bin verify .kolm artifacts (CID/HMAC receipt chain), not the audit report; Swift/Kotlin verify HMAC receipts. The verify-everywhere claim is currently aspirational. This is the central gap behind inventions #1-3. Plan.

verifiedProposals.title: Homebrew + winget install paths are non-functional
verifiedProposals.reason: Confirmed: all-zero sha256 in kolm.rb and both winget installers, pointing at unpublished github release/tarball URLs, so installs fail with checksum errors. Real. Proper fix needs release infra (plan); interim de-listing could be immediate.

verifiedProposals.title: No developer-facing page documents the SDK/CLI/integration reach
verifiedProposals.reason: Verified: docs.html #cli-sdk is a 5-line snippet, integrations.html is data connectors, platform/compiler pages only repeat 'no SDK rewrite', and no offline-verify code sample exists site-wide. The 8-language reach and MCP/adapters are invisible to developers. Accurate gap; addressed by invention #3. Plan.

verifiedProposals.title: Python framework adapters are not installable
verifiedProposals.reason: FALSE: packages/python-langchain-kolm/pyproject.toml and packages/python-llamaindex-kolm/pyproject.toml both exist and are pip-installable (kolm-langchain / kolm-llamaindex, peer-deps wired). The gap does not exist. Reject as inaccurate.

verifiedProposals.title: On-device SDK inference backends are stubs that throw
verifiedProposals.reason: Verified: Kolm.swift defines noRuntimeAvailable/runtimeFailure and backends require a vendored runtime; Kotlin backends throw. predict() does not return text out-of-the-box, so the file-header claim over-promises. The minimal honest fix is improvement #11 (ship-now caveat); actually wiring CoreML/ExecuTorch/llama.cpp is large and out of scope, so the broader gap is plan.

verifiedProposals.title: TUI :run is a mock, not real inference
verifiedProposals.reason: Verified: cli/kolm-tui.mjs mockInfer() does substring example-matching and returns a '(local mock)' stub; comments say wave 123 will wire /v1/wrap/verified. Accurate gap and the code is upfront about being a mock, but wiring real inference into the TUI/:serve is non-trivial and lower priority than the moat/constraint items. Plan.

verifiedProposals.title: SDK version skew signals immaturity
verifiedProposals.reason: Confirmed: sdk/node 0.1.0 vs package 0.2.6, sdk/go 0.1.0, sdk/python 0.3.0, cli 0.2.6. Real and trivially fixable; same as improvement #7. Ship now.

currentState: Backend is a Node/ESM monolith: server.js mounts a single 1.4 MB src/router.js via buildRouter(); 604 .js files under src/. I built an import graph (static + dynamic string imports + template-literal registry loaders + adapter string-maps) resolved against actual file paths. Reachability from the server/router graph: 477 of 604 modules referenced; 105 not statically reachable. Cross-referencing tests/ (562 test files importing 400 src modules via ../src/*.js), the 105 split into 70 unwired-but-tested (real surface-B capabilities with coverage that are simply not HTTP-mounted) and 34 unwired+untested (strongest dead/orphan signal). Spot-reads confirm the latter are mostly surface-B/CLI library code (init-agent.js W238 scaffolder, mesh.js W237 tailnet planner, usage-analytics.js W265 whose own header claims dashboard+CLI+SDK consume it but nothing in src imports it, pipeline-make/ship/train, quantize-bakeoff, repo-codegraph, tool-runtime). zero of these verbs appear in router.js and they are not bundled by name in cli/kolm.js, so they are genuinely disconnected from the running product, not deletable as dead until wiring intent is confirmed. There is a 'floating subgraph': modules like cloud-providers/modal.js import each other (cloud/runpod.js, deployment-plans.js, device-adapters/*) but the island never connects to router.js. Duplication: src/proxy.js (191 lines, W808 signature-on-capture, tested) vs src/services/proxy.js (334 lines, W240 standalone CLI capture proxy with shebang, used by scripts/dogfood-proxy.ps1) are different concerns sharing a name; src/services.js documents a deliberate 3-process split (redactor/compiler/proxy) exec'd directly as container entrypoints, so src/services/* are legitimate unwired entrypoints not dupes. benchmark family overlaps (benchmark.js 218 / benchmarks.js 224 / benchmark-compare.js 587 / bench-harness.js 1080 / bench-report-md.js 146). modal has three impls (cloud-providers/modal 10.8KB, compute/backends/modal 3.5KB, device-adapters/modal-adapter 0.9KB) but they are distinct layers (provider/backend/adapter), reachable via registries, not redundant. RISK SCAN: shipped public/*.html is CLEAN of every FORBIDDEN substring, case-sensitive (grep RC=1) and case-insensitive near-misses are only legitimate framework-mapping copy (EU AI Act Art.12, SOC 2, HIPAA legal text in baa.html) which do not contain the exact forbidden phrases (EU AI Act compliant / HIPAA-ready / PHI never leaves / BAA boundary). report.html keeps sig__ok (1 occurrence). SECRETS: .env and .env.* are ignored in .gitignore/.dockerignore/.railwayignore; only .env.example is git-tracked; the only console output naming secrets logs their ABSENCE (artifact.js:161, router.js:1127 RECIPE_RECEIPT_SECRET not set) - no secret VALUE echo; ed25519.js returns privateKey only to in-process callers, never to res.json/console. public/ contains only PUBLIC keys (verify.html sample keys). Dependencies are lean: 15 deps, 4 devDeps, type=module, engines node>=20.
sota: 2026 SOTA for JS/ESM dead-code + module-graph hygiene is Knip (knip.dev): unused files/exports/deps/types, 80+ framework plugins with auto entry-point detection, ESM-native, --fix auto-removal, CI-gateable. It is the consensus best-in-class over depcheck/ts-prune/madge/unimported (repowise comparison 2026). This repo has NONE of these wired: package.json has no knip/depcheck/madge; eslint.config.js only enforces in-file no-unused-vars (3 blocks), which cannot see module-level orphans or the unwired-from-router island - exactly the gap my manual graph surfaced. For the secret/supply-chain dimension, 2026 norms are gitleaks/trufflehog pre-commit + SLSA provenance on artifacts (the repo even has an unwired src/slsa-provenance.js) and signed SBOMs (unwired src/sbom-emit referenced earlier) - the trust-moat story for surface A would be materially stronger if dead-code/secret gates were themselves part of the signed build provenance.\n\nSources: https://knip.dev/ , https://www.repowise.dev/blog/comparisons/best-dead-code-detection-tools , https://effectivetypescript.com/2023/07/29/knip/

verifiedProposals.title: Signed wiring-coverage manifest in the build provenance (route+test reachability as a trust artifact)
verifiedProposals.reason: Verified real: src/ed25519.js (loadOrCreateDefaultSigner), src/merkle.js + transparency-log.js (persistCheckpoint/getTransparencyLog), and tmp/orphan_scan2.cjs (runs, reports 105 orphans) all exist. Buildable. Caveat: anchoring an INTERNAL hygiene manifest in the SAME customer-facing transparency log that carries ASR readiness reports risks diluting the moat artifact's meaning; gate it as a separate origin and use the env fa56 key (not the create-if-missing path) so no secret is echoed. Plan, not ship-now, due to that semantic-scope decision.

verifiedProposals.title: 'Declared-vs-actual wiring' lint that reads module-header capability claims
verifiedProposals.reason: Seed cases verified verbatim: src/usage-analytics.js header literally claims 'the dashboard, the CLI ... and the ... SDK all read from one module' yet zero src/ files reference it; proxy.js header points HTTP wiring at router.js. Buildable as a .cjs regex+graph diff. But regex over freeform comment prose is brittle/low-precision (high false-positive risk), so value is modest; plan.

verifiedProposals.title: Repo-wide FORBIDDEN-substring guard extended to ALL customer surfaces with a signed clean-attestation
verifiedProposals.reason: Grounded: public/*.html is currently CLEAN of all 19 forbidden substrings, but 'pip install kolm' leaks into packages/sdk-python and sdk/python (confirmed). Constraint is binding everywhere, so extending the verbatim case-sensitive scan to sdk/packages/cli is correct and small. Surgical, signer primitives exist. Ship-now (sign the clean record as its own origin, never echo the key).

verifiedProposals.title: Fix the 'pip install kolm' forbidden-substring leak in the Python SDK
verifiedProposals.reason: Confirmed at packages/sdk-python/README.md:16, packages/sdk-python/kolm/__init__.py:243-245 (printed to SDK users), sdk/python/PUBLISHING.md:65, sdk/python/pyproject.toml:28 (incl. [gguf]/[onnx]/[transformers] and ==0.2.0 variants which still contain the exact forbidden substring). This is a binding case-sensitive constraint violation on PyPI-rendered surfaces. Trivial replace. Highest-priority ship-now.

verifiedProposals.title: Add a quarantine/ALLOWLIST for the 34 unwired+untested modules instead of deleting
verifiedProposals.reason: Listed orphans exist (init-agent.js, mesh.js, usage-analytics.js, pipeline-*.js, quantize-bakeoff.js, repo-codegraph.js, tool-runtime.js, etc. all verified on disk). Respects the no-delete constraint by freezing the set in data/orphan-allowlist.json. Pairs with the coverage gate. Low-risk, additive, ship-now; verify each entry's intent tag against the header before committing.

verifiedProposals.title: Stop tracking 85 MB of QA screenshots and multi-MB research dumps in git
verifiedProposals.reason: Confirmed: git ls-files qa = 92 tracked files; deploy ignores (.vercelignore/.railwayignore) mean no ship impact. git rm --cached is safe and keeps the working tree. Plan (not ship-now) only because the task is no-git-push and rewriting tracked-history hygiene is an operator decision; the .gitignore edit itself is fine to stage.

verifiedProposals.title: Document the canonical benchmark module and the proxy name-collision
verifiedProposals.reason: All six files exist (benchmark.js/benchmarks.js/benchmark-compare.js/bench-harness.js; proxy.js W808 vs services/proxy.js W240 - headers verified). Pure comment-banner additions, zero behavior risk. Low impact but trivially safe; ship-now.

verifiedProposals.title: Adopt Knip with curated entry points as the CI dead-code gate
verifiedProposals.reason: Verified no knip/depcheck/madge in package.json; eslint only no-unused-vars. Buildable. Caveat: Knip will mis-handle this tree's template-literal loaders + adapter string-maps + the 2.5MB cli/kolm.js, so it duplicates the bespoke resolver the manifest invention already provides. Report-mode only, never auto-delete (constraint). Plan, and likely redundant with the signed manifest gate; pick one.

verifiedProposals.title: FORBIDDEN substring 'pip install kolm' leaks into user-facing SDK distribution surfaces (outside public/ but customer-visible)
verifiedProposals.reason: Verified accurate at every cited location; public/*.html is genuinely clean, so this is the real exposure. Binding case-sensitive constraint applies to all customer-visible surfaces (PyPI README + runtime error string). Correctly identified gap; fix immediately.

verifiedProposals.title: No automated dead-code / orphan-module gate; eslint cannot see module-level orphans
verifiedProposals.reason: Confirmed: eslint.config.js sets no-unused-vars at lines 50/79/107 only; no module-graph tool. orphan_scan2.cjs reports 105 unreachable-from-router modules, matching the claim. Real gap; remediation is the manifest gate or Knip (plan).

verifiedProposals.title: Disconnected surface-B 'floating subgraph': built capability never wired to any route
verifiedProposals.reason: Substantiated: usage-analytics.js header claims dashboard+CLI+SDK consumers but zero src/ imports it; pipeline-*/repo-codegraph/quantize-bakeoff/tool-runtime exist and are unwired. Credibility risk if a buyer audits the tree; plan (allowlist + wire-or-retire).
